value.Title: Green tax incentive boosts film industry
value.Overview: McDonough County Voice (Illinois), NEWS; Pg. A1, 507words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ7-HG13-RTY2-V14V-00000-00Green tax incentive boosts film industry2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisNEWSPg. A1Green tax incentive boosts film industryByJanis ReeserSpringfield State Journal-Register

Illinois film and television productions that meet green certification standards can now earn an extra 5% tax credit, making the state the first in the nation to offer such an incentive, according to a community announcement.

The initiative, launched in celebration of Earth Day, is expected to boost both the entertainment industry and the state's green economy, according to the announcement.

Productions recognized as a "certified green production" through the Illinois Film Office must meet environmental benchmarks such as reducing waste, lowering energy use and emissions, utilizing sustainable materials and implementing responsible food practices.

Illinois film industry

sees record growth

The announcement comes as Illinois' film industry continues to grow, with production expenditures reaching a record-breaking $703 million in 2025 and supporting an estimated 18,000 industry hires. The expansion of the tax credit is expected to drive demand for clean energy infrastructure, such as battery systems, solar-powered trailers and renewable energy solutions, creating new jobs across the state.

Chicago now ranks as the third-best big city for those who work in the film industry, according to the announcement.

Officials applaud Illinois film industry expansion

"This expansion reflects our commitment to growing Illinois' economy in a way that is both competitive and sustainable," Kristin Richards, director of the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity, said in the announcement. "We are attracting world-class productions while building the infrastructure and workforce needed for a cleaner future."

The new incentive is also expected to drive growth in Illinois' clean energy and green vendor sectors. As productions adapt to meet certification requirements, demand will increase for infrastructure such as battery systems, solar-powered trailers and renewable energy solutions - in turn creating new, good-paying jobs across the state.

"As someone who has worked for years to help make productions in Illinois more sustainable, I am absolutely thrilled that Governor Pritzker and the Illinois Film Office have made Illinois the first state in the country to offer productions a meaningful incentive to go green," Ellen White, CEO and founder of Ecofixr, an Illinois-based full-service sustainability services company, said in the announcement. "This is going to be a gamechanger for Illinois and will undoubtedly help Illinois attract more productions and the green jobs that go with them."

In addition to long-term infrastructure growth, more immediate changes on set are expected, such as transitioning from paper scripts to digital tablets, replacing single-use plastic water bottles with refill stations, and reducing reliance on diesel in favor of cleaner energy alternatives.

value.Title: Summit Ridge Energy and Black Bear Energy Mark Completion of a New Illinois Shines Community Solar Rooftop Project Hosted by LBA Logistics
value.Overview: Professional Services Close-Up, 848words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ6-21H3-RXJ3-J17R-00000-00Summit Ridge Energy and Black Bear Energy Mark Completion of a New Illinois Shines Community Solar Rooftop Project Hosted by LBA Logistics2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisSummit Ridge Energy and Black Bear Energy Mark Completion of a New Illinois Shines Community Solar Rooftop Project Hosted by LBA Logistics

Summit Ridge Energy, a commercial solar and energy storage company, on May 7 held a ribbon-cutting ceremony in Melrose Park, Ill., to celebrate the completion of a 1.62 MW rooftop community solar project.

Developed in collaboration with LBA Logistics (LBA), a full-service real estate investment and management company, and Black Bear Energy, a commercial buyer's representative specializing in onsite renewable energy and cleantech services, the groups said the project representsa significant milestone for equitableclean energy development in Illinois.

According to a release, hosted on an industrial building owned by LBA, the Melrose Park installation will generate over 2 million kWh of renewable energy annually, the equivalent of powering 200 homes each year. Made possible through the Illinois Shines Adjustable Block Program, the projectparticipatesin the Traditional Community Solar (TCS) program, delivering distributed energy directly to the grid. Project subscribers, including low-to-moderate income (LMI) households, receive a utility bill credit, typically saving 10-20 percent on their electricity costs - all without installing solar panels on their own property.

The rooftop solar project sits within both an Illinois Environmental Justice Community (EJC) and a federally designated Energy Community, a combination that makes this project a particularly powerful example of equitable energy development. Illinois designates EJCs as areas where residents face a disproportionate burden of environmental harm, typically due to a legacy of heavy industrial activity, air and water pollution, and underinvestment in infrastructure. The Illinois Shines program prioritizes such areas, aiming to expand access to clean energy and associated economic benefits.

In addition to delivering affordable energy, the project creates local jobs during construction and maintenance, strengthens Illinois' electric grid through reliable distributed generation, and earns a Rooftop Adder under Illinois Shines, an incentive for utilizing existing rooftop space instead of undeveloped land.

Summit Ridge Energy is a major company in Illinois' solar industry, with more than 553 MW of community solar projects across the state and over 3 GW nationwide -- both operating and in development.

The groups added the project represents the latest milestone in a decade-long partnership between LBA and Black Bear Energy that began in 2015. Over the past ten years, the teams have collaborated on a broad portfolio of community solar projects, including 17.5 MW across four Maryland industrial properties owned by LBA, with the solar systems developed, owned, and operated by Summit Ridge Energy under the Maryland Community Solar Program. The program provides locally generated energy at reduced rates to Maryland residents under the Maryland Community Solar Program. Additionally, LBA recently energized two Illinois properties totaling 5.5 MW of community solar, including the 1.62 MW Melrose Park facility.

The ribbon-cutting ceremony was attended by local officials and representatives from Summit Ridge Energy, LBA, Black Bear Energy, and ComEd.

Michelle German, Vice President of Portfolio Operations & Sustainability at LBA, commented, "How we own and operate our assets reflects our responsibility as long-term owners and the value we strive to create in every community where we do business is approached with thoughtful, long-term planning. By hosting community solar on our industrial rooftops, we're expanding local energy access to LMI households and supporting job creation in the surrounding community. This is not part of a standalone effort, but part of how we approach ownership and operations every day."

Echoing these sentiments, Victoria Stulgis, President of Black Bear Energy, emphasized the value of long-term collaboration, stating, "Projects like this happen with trust built over years of honest, hard work together. LBA has been one of our most valued partners for a decade; they bring the vision, the portfolio, and the commitment, and they treat Black Bear as a true extension of their team. That kind of relationship is rare, andit'swhat allows us to move quickly and confidently on complex projects like this. Watching 1.62 MW come online in Melrose Park, delivering real savings to real families, is exactly why we do this work."

"Illinois has become one of the most exciting community solar markets in the country," saidBrent Buchberger, VP of Project Developmentat Summit Ridge Energy. "The Illinois Shines program is working - bringing affordable, locally generated energy to communities that have historically shouldered the burden of pollution without sharing insolar's benefits. With LBA and Black Bear Energy as partners who share our long-term commitment, this rooftop project in an Environmental Justice Community is a strong example of what's possible. We'reproud to see it energized and look forward to many more.

urn:contentItem:6JHY-K3T3-RRNM-H399-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Caterpillar reports 6.1% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Caterpillar reports 6.1% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit

AMERICAN RESULTS

Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT), announced net profit of $2.5b for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 6.1% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 27% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 27.2% from $2.0b to $2.5b

- Total revenue was up 22.2% from $14.2b to $17.4b

- EPS growth 30.3% from $4.22 to $5.5

- EBIT to Sales was up from 18% to 18.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 18% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 14.1% to 14.6%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was down from 4% to 3.8%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 30.3%, Revenue was up 22.2% and Net Profit was up 27.2%.

ACTIVITIES:

Caterpillar Inc., headquartered in Deerfield, Illinois, is a global leader in the manufacturing and sale of construction and mining equipment, diesel and natural gas engines, industrial turbines, and locomotives. Founded in 1925, the company operates through four primary segments: Construction Industries, Resource Industries, Energy & Transportation, and Financial Products, serving diverse markets including infrastructure, energy, and transportation. Its core activities encompass designing, producing, and distributing machinery for earthmoving, material handling, and power generation, while also offering aftermarket parts, services, and financing solutions to enhance customer productivity. Among its fastest-growing segments are Energy & Transportation, driven by rising demand for power systems in renewable energy projects and data centers, and digital solutions like Cat Connect, which leverage IoT for equipment monitoring and predictive maintenance. This strategic focus on innovation and sustainability positions Caterpillar to capitalize on global infrastructure investments and environmental regulations.

MARKET POSITION:

Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT), the NYSE's largest Industrial Machinery company by market cap, soared $320.17 (or 56.1%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $890.62. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 5.3% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 50.8%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $2,688 [vs $1,432 for the S&P 500 Index], for a capital gain of $1,615 and dividend reinvested of $72. The average annualised return to shareholders has been 63.9% in the past two years. The average daily volume of 1.8 million shares so far in 2026 was 0.7 times the average daily volume in 2025.

Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT $897.45), the NYSE's largest Industrial Machinery company by market cap, has jumped $177.36 (or 24.6%) in the month ended May 31 to close at $897.45. This gain makes the stock the highest rising one in the Industrial Machinery sector which is up 4.9%. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 616.1 points (or 9.1%) in the month, this represented a relative price increase of 15.6%.

The volume in the month was 0.8 times the average trading of 53.1 million shares per month.

Uptrend

-- Today's Volatility:

The 4% discount to 12-month high of $931.35 against the 181% premium to 12-month low of $319.85 suggests the stock continues to push for new highs.

Activities

Caterpillar Inc., headquartered in Deerfield, Illinois, is a global leader in the manufacturing and sale of construction and mining equipment, diesel and natural gas engines, industrial turbines, and locomotives. Founded in 1925, the company operates through four primary segments: Construction Industries, Resource Industries, Energy & Transportation, and Financial Products, serving diverse markets including infrastructure, energy, and transportation. Its core activities encompass designing, producing, and distributing machinery for earthmoving, material handling, and power generation, while also offering aftermarket parts, services, and financing solutions to enhance customer productivity. Among its fastest-growing segments are Energy & Transportation, driven by rising demand for power systems in renewable energy projects and data centers, and digital solutions like Cat Connect, which leverage IoT for equipment monitoring and predictive maintenance. This strategic focus on innovation and sustainability positions Caterpillar to capitalize on global infrastructure investments and environmental regulations. It is the NYSE's largest Industrial machinery company by market capitalisation.

Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT) stands as a global leader in the manufacturing of construction and mining equipment, offering a diversified range of products that cater to various industry needs. Its portfolio includes Caterpillar excavators, Cat hydraulic excavators, and Caterpillar wheel loaders, which are pivotal in Caterpillar earthmoving equipment. The company is renowned for providing Cat construction equipment such as Caterpillar bulldozers, Caterpillar motor graders, and Caterpillar backhoe loaders. Moreover, it excels in supplying Caterpillar mining trucks, Cat off-highway trucks, and Cat articulated trucks for large-scale projects. In the power systems domain, Caterpillar diesel engines, Cat generator sets, and Caterpillar power systems underscore its comprehensive offerings. Caterpillar engine parts, Cat fuel systems, and Caterpillar aftertreatment systems are essential components for long-term machinery efficiency. Additionally, the company supports the industry with Cat aftermarket parts, Caterpillar remanufactured parts, and Caterpillar undercarriage parts, offering solutions like Cat telehandlers, Cat compact track loaders, and Cat skid steer loaders. Its commitment extends to Caterpillar marine engines, Cat electric drive mining trucks, Caterpillar portable generators, Cat heavy machinery rental, and Caterpillar powertrain components, ensuring versatile applications across sectors.

Its core business activities encompass gaming, hospitality, entertainment, and dining experiences, designed to deliver premium guest services and drive revenue through diversified offerings. In recent years, MGM has seen rapid growth in digital segments, particularly online sports betting and iGaming via its BetMGM platform, which has expanded through strategic partnerships and technological innovations. Additionally, international expansion and integrated resort developments have emerged as fast-growing areas, capitalizing on rising demand for experiential leisure and contributing to the company's overall revenue diversification and market resilience. It is the NYSE's 3rd largest Gambling company by market capitalisation.

MGM Resorts International (NYSE:MGM) stands as a dominant force in the gaming and hospitality industry, renowned for its iconic Las Vegas destinations like MGM Grand Las Vegas, BellagioLas Vegas, and Aria Resort & Casino. Bolstered by strategic initiatives, including the MGM shareholder agreement and proactive MGM corporate governance, the company emphasizes its commitment to shareholder value. MGM Resorts' voting agreement and board seats guarantee enhance its governance structure, reflecting proactive MGM shareholder activism. For investors, NYSE:MGM stock represents a strong play in entertainment, supported by the lucrative MGM Rewards loyalty program and thriving MGM entertainment residencies. The company's diversified portfolio, including MGM dining restaurants and resort packages, further solidifies its market positioning. As MGM continues to evolve, its takeover defense and comprehensive proxy statement underscore a robust strategic approach. Regular MGM investor updates highlight its focus on growth potential, while the MGM annual meeting and shareholder vote remain pivotal to shaping its future trajectory.

urn:contentItem:6JHV-2T23-S4GG-D53C-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Caterpillar EPS increases 7% Corporate Wire Date: 01 May 2026 12:35 EDT Release Date: April 30, 20262026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Caterpillar EPS increases 7% Corporate Wire Date: 01 May 2026 12:35 EDT Release Date: April 30, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT), announced EPS of $5.5 for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 6.8% sequentially from $5.15 in the previous quarter [Q4/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 27.2% from $2.0b to $2.5b

- Total revenue was up 22.2% from $14.2b to $17.4b

- EPS growth 30.3% from $4.22 to $5.5

- EBIT to Sales was up from 18% to 18.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 18% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 14.1% to 14.6%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was down from 4% to 3.8%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Dec 202530 Sep 2025EPS, $5.55.24.9Sequential growth in EPS %6.84.95.8Revenue, $ billion17.419.117.6Sequential growth in Revenue %-9.08.56.5Net Profit, $ billion2.52.42.3Sequential growth in Net Profit %6.14.45.6

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 30.3%, Revenue was up 22.2% and Net Profit was up 27.2%.

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, $5.54.2PCP growth in EPS %30-27Revenue, $ billion17.414.2PCP growth in Revenue %22.2-9.8Net Profit, $ billion2.52.0PCP growth in Net Profit %27.2-29.9

ACTIVITIES:

Caterpillar Inc., headquartered in Deerfield, Illinois, is a global leader in the manufacturing and sale of construction and mining equipment, diesel and natural gas engines, industrial turbines, and locomotives. Founded in 1925, the company operates through four primary segments: Construction Industries, Resource Industries, Energy & Transportation, and Financial Products, serving diverse markets including infrastructure, energy, and transportation. Its core activities encompass designing, producing, and distributing machinery for earthmoving, material handling, and power generation, while also offering aftermarket parts, services, and financing solutions to enhance customer productivity. Among its fastest-growing segments are Energy & Transportation, driven by rising demand for power systems in renewable energy projects and data centers, and digital solutions like Cat Connect, which leverage IoT for equipment monitoring and predictive maintenance. This strategic focus on innovation and sustainability positions Caterpillar to capitalize on global infrastructure investments and environmental regulations.

MARKET POSITION:

Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT), the NYSE's largest Industrial Machinery company by market cap, soared $320.17 (or 56.1%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $890.62. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 5.3% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 50.8%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $2,688 [vs $1,432 for the S&P 500 Index], for a capital gain of $1,615 and dividend reinvested of $72. The average annualised return to shareholders has been 63.9% in the past two years. The average daily volume of 1.8 million shares so far in 2026 was 0.7 times the average daily volume in 2025.

urn:contentItem:6JJ0-7JB3-SCSG-X0BT-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CHICAGO BUILDER CLAYCO BETS ON SOLAR AND STORAGE WITH NEW NATIONAL ARM

Clayco, a Chicago-based contractor, is expanding into solar and storage with a new national division, announced on 4th March 2026. The venture, known as Clayco Power and Energy, aims to create nearly 1,000 jobs nationwide by 2027 and generate about $300 million in annual revenue.The unit will oversee design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and storage projects and is already advancing a 350-acre Manteno, Illinois site, with construction slated to begin this June.The expansion comes as U.S. electricity demand is forecast to rise 15%–20% by 2030, with solar and storage contributing a growing share of new capacity after accounting for roughly 85% of last year’s additions.

+ IDAHO NATIONAL LAB TO HOST NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER: CLAYCO TO LEAD DOE PROPOSAL DESIGN-BUILD EFFORT

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.In its advisory role, Clayco will align design and construction approaches with the operational requirements of high-density AI workloads, leveraging its integrated delivery model to meet the fast, reliable, and precise needs of modern data centers. The consortium brings together organizations with expertise in advanced nuclear technology, energy systems, and risk modeling, complementing Clayco's practical delivery capabilities. Deep Atomic's approach combines advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to ensure reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The collaboration aims to establish a scalable platform that could position the U.S. as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco helping integrate innovative energy solutions and execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ FOUNTAIN PARK AS BEACON OF RENEWAL: CLAYCO'S CLARK CALLS FOR COLLABORATIVE RECOVERY

Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark says the business community must join with city leaders to forge a unified rebuilding strategy for areas that faced long-standing struggles. In Fountain Park, he voiced cautious optimism about renewal and noted the visible damage from the disaster.Clark said Clayco acted promptly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Spencer, city services, and local partners. The company’s commitment to long-term rebuilding includes funding and staffing programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward, complemented by ongoing community support.Describing Fountain Park as a potential beacon of hope, Clark highlighted preexisting programs that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes, such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority. He emphasized that real recovery starts with the civic community and requires a shared will to move forward, adding that progress happens incrementally—one house at a time, one block at a time.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [2 News That Matters]

+ MERITAX ADVISORS JOINS RYAN COMPANIES TO ACCELERATE PROPERTY TAX SOLUTIONS

Ryan Companies expanded its property tax services with the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, announced official on the 10th of February 2026. Meritax, a Texas-based commercial property tax consultant, has supported clients across various real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value in Texas and beyond.The deal is designed to boost Ryan's capabilities in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services, while incorporating Meritax's analytics-driven approach and technology-enabled advocacy to keep pace with evolving property tax systems. Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, said the addition brings a disciplined, client-focused team with deep Texas knowledge to the firm.As part of the integration, Meritax partners including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with about 20 other team members, will join Ryan. The move adds to Ryan’s global footprint, which already serves more than 74,000 clients with a workforce of over 7,100 professionals, reinforcing the firm’s leading position in business taxes.

+ VETERANS DAY FEATURE: ANNA GRIFFIN CONNECTS MILITARY SERVICE TO CONSTRUCTION EXCELLENCE AT RYAN

Anna Griffin, director of construction for Ryan Companies US, sat down on the AZ Big Podcast to discuss how her military background informs leadership and resilience in the construction industry. The interview, recorded 5 days ago, comes as Veterans Day is observed in Arizona and highlights Griffin's trajectory from Air Force service to overseeing Southwest construction projects. Griffin recently returned from a seven-month deployment to Jordan as aircraft maintenance squadron commander for the 165th Airlift Wing, and she notes that the two fields share common ground: "Construction has so many parallels to my military service," she noted. "You’re still serving — just in a different way. You’re building something lasting, something that supports people and communities." At Ryan Companies, Griffin guides project management teams across healthcare, multifamily housing, and education. After nearly four years at the firm, her leadership helps navigate the complexity of construction environments, with a focus on integrity, accountability, and mission readiness, qualities that served her in the military and now guide civilian projects. The AZ Big Podcast, hosted by Michael Gossie and Amy Lindsey, is sponsored by Burch & Cracchiolo and aims to spotlight how strong leadership and collaboration drive success across industries in Arizona's business landscape. Griffin's episode is timely as the state honors its veterans and recognizes the valuable skills they contribute to the workforce. Listeners are encouraged to tune in to hear her compelling story and understand how veterans like her are making meaningful contributions to sectors such as construction. As she continues her work with Ryan Companies, her journey demonstrates the strength and adaptability of veterans transitioning into fresh roles, underscoring that the capabilities developed in military service are transferable across fields.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHR-KV33-S2KX-K4YG-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Caterpillar reports 6.1% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Caterpillar reports 6.1% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit

AMERICAN RESULTS

Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT), announced net profit of $2.5b for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 6.1% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 27% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 27.2% from $2.0b to $2.5b

- Total revenue was up 22.2% from $14.2b to $17.4b

- EPS growth 30.3% from $4.22 to $5.5

- EBIT to Sales was up from 18% to 18.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 18% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 14.1% to 14.6%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was down from 4% to 3.8%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Dec 202530 Sep 2025EPS, $5.55.24.9Sequential growth in EPS %6.84.95.8Revenue, $ billion17.419.117.6Sequential growth in Revenue %-9.08.56.5Net Profit, $ billion2.52.42.3Sequential growth in Net Profit %6.14.45.6

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 30.3%, Revenue was up 22.2% and Net Profit was up 27.2%.

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, $5.54.2PCP growth in EPS %30-27Revenue, $ billion17.414.2PCP growth in Revenue %22.2-9.8Net Profit, $ billion2.52.0PCP growth in Net Profit %27.2-29.9

ACTIVITIES:

Caterpillar Inc., headquartered in Deerfield, Illinois, is a global leader in the manufacturing and sale of construction and mining equipment, diesel and natural gas engines, industrial turbines, and locomotives. Founded in 1925, the company operates through four primary segments: Construction Industries, Resource Industries, Energy & Transportation, and Financial Products, serving diverse markets including infrastructure, energy, and transportation. Its core activities encompass designing, producing, and distributing machinery for earthmoving, material handling, and power generation, while also offering aftermarket parts, services, and financing solutions to enhance customer productivity. Among its fastest-growing segments are Energy & Transportation, driven by rising demand for power systems in renewable energy projects and data centers, and digital solutions like Cat Connect, which leverage IoT for equipment monitoring and predictive maintenance. This strategic focus on innovation and sustainability positions Caterpillar to capitalize on global infrastructure investments and environmental regulations.

MARKET POSITION:

Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT), the NYSE's largest Industrial Machinery company by market cap, soared $320.17 (or 56.1%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $890.62. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 5.3% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 50.8%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $2,688 [vs $1,432 for the S&P 500 Index], for a capital gain of $1,615 and dividend reinvested of $72. The average annualised return to shareholders has been 63.9% in the past two years. The average daily volume of 1.8 million shares so far in 2026 was 0.7 times the average daily volume in 2025.

urn:contentItem:6JHN-4F83-RSSF-N0CV-00000-00Dhruv Patel: Power and Energy Leader Mentors Emerging Talent2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:54ZLexisNexisSEND TO LEXIS NEXISDhruv Patel: Power and Energy Leader Mentors Emerging TalentBack To:ENR 2026 Top 20 Under 40

Dhruv Patel

39, President, Renewable Energy and Energy Storage McCarthy Building Cos.

Phoenix

A nationally recognized leader in the power and energy sector, Patel has nearly two decades of experience in utility operations, renewable development and large-scale construction. He holds a bachelor's degree in electrical engineering and an MBA from Arizona State University, which he credits with helping him bridge technical expertise and business leadership. Patel began his career at utility Arizona Public Service, spending eight years in key roles across distribution and transmission operations, power procurement and resource program management. He led its utility-owned solar program, delivering 200 MW of solar assets.

Since joining McCarthy in 2013, Patel has helped grow the firm workforce to include 1,500 craft workers, delivering nearly 20 GW of solar capacity and more than 12 GWh of energy storage across 18 states. Serving on McCarthy's National Leadership Team, he actively mentors emerging leaders and supports apprenticeship and craft training programs. Within the industry, Patel also is on the national advisory council of the Energy Storage Association and remains active in the Solar Energy Industry Association and other industry organizations. His project resume includes the 800-MW Double Black Diamond Solar project in Illinois, the 250-MW Gateway Energy Storage project in California and the $2-billion Sun Streams Energy Complex in Arizona, among others.

How did you break into the industry?

Very early in my career while working at Arizona Public Service, and before solar was common, I unintentionally volunteered to lead the utility's efforts to prepare the grid to incorporate renewable energy into its operations. Purely by luck and coincidence, I became engaged in the process of bringing renewable energy to the state by engineering and managing the evaluation and supporting establishment of utility grid tie-in requirements, developing technical PPA terms and conditions, and over time, helping build systems to support the utility's resource management program. That work expanded into permitting, financial analysis and development and construction of multiple solar projects, ultimately helping execute the utility's renewable energy strategy. This experience put me on my renewable energy and construction career trajectory.

What is your favorite part of your work?

The work I've been involved with over the past several years at McCarthy has been the most gratifying of my career. My role as a coach, advisor, mediator and mentor allows me to help others develop their leadership skills, communication skills and problem-solving skills. By investing here not only are all our projects better and achieving better results for our clients, but I am also involved in developing trusted leaders who value each other's perspectives and push one another to innovate, improve and do better, which benefits individual projects as well as our industry. It has been incredible to be part of this people-centric development initiative, and to see where our industry is headed.

Where do you see yourself in 10 years, and what goals have you set for yourself to get there?

In ten years, I hope to be involved in shaping McCarthy's broader business strategies and enterprise initiatives while also contributing to how our industry continues to evolve. This would include furthering the work I am doing today, that involves developing future leaders along with shaping McCarthy's national efforts beyond the Renewable Energy group. We're creating a model that we think can be successfully replicated in other market sectors.

Through my experience leading McCarthy's national Renewable Energy group, I am consciously developing and building upon my skills and knowledge about enterprise growth, and plan to incrementally bring the practices we're implementing with our team to other teams within McCarthy while staying engaged externally with partners, clients and industry peers to help advance best practices.

How do you maintain a work-life balance?

I have a daily meditation and exercise practice [and that] allows me to decompress, have clearer thinking and to be intentional and present. I also set healthy boundaries that protect my time at work, time with family and friends and taking care of myself so I can show up for everyone as my best self.

What is a piece of advice you'd like to share with other young professionals in this industry, or those coming up behind you?

I frequently hear young professionals talking about their goals of achieving titles. My advice is that they shouldn't chase titles. Instead, chase skills and knowledge and let the titles chase you.

urn:contentItem:6JHS-8DS3-RRN7-S4GM-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CHICAGO BUILDER CLAYCO BETS ON SOLAR AND STORAGE WITH NEW NATIONAL ARM

Clayco, a Chicago-based contractor, is expanding into solar and storage with a new national division, announced on 4th March 2026. The venture, known as Clayco Power and Energy, aims to create nearly 1,000 jobs nationwide by 2027 and generate about $300 million in annual revenue.The unit will oversee design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and storage projects and is already advancing a 350-acre Manteno, Illinois site, with construction slated to begin this June.The expansion comes as U.S. electricity demand is forecast to rise 15%–20% by 2030, with solar and storage contributing a growing share of new capacity after accounting for roughly 85% of last year’s additions.

+ IDAHO NATIONAL LAB TO HOST NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER: CLAYCO TO LEAD DOE PROPOSAL DESIGN-BUILD EFFORT

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.In its advisory role, Clayco will align design and construction approaches with the operational requirements of high-density AI workloads, leveraging its integrated delivery model to meet the fast, reliable, and precise needs of modern data centers. The consortium brings together organizations with expertise in advanced nuclear technology, energy systems, and risk modeling, complementing Clayco's practical delivery capabilities. Deep Atomic's approach combines advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to ensure reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The collaboration aims to establish a scalable platform that could position the U.S. as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco helping integrate innovative energy solutions and execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ FOUNTAIN PARK AS BEACON OF RENEWAL: CLAYCO'S CLARK CALLS FOR COLLABORATIVE RECOVERY

Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark says the business community must join with city leaders to forge a unified rebuilding strategy for areas that faced long-standing struggles. In Fountain Park, he voiced cautious optimism about renewal and noted the visible damage from the disaster.Clark said Clayco acted promptly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Spencer, city services, and local partners. The company’s commitment to long-term rebuilding includes funding and staffing programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward, complemented by ongoing community support.Describing Fountain Park as a potential beacon of hope, Clark highlighted preexisting programs that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes, such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority. He emphasized that real recovery starts with the civic community and requires a shared will to move forward, adding that progress happens incrementally—one house at a time, one block at a time.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [2 News That Matters]

+ MERITAX ADVISORS JOINS RYAN COMPANIES TO ACCELERATE PROPERTY TAX SOLUTIONS

Ryan Companies expanded its property tax services with the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, announced official on the 10th of February 2026. Meritax, a Texas-based commercial property tax consultant, has supported clients across various real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value in Texas and beyond.The deal is designed to boost Ryan's capabilities in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services, while incorporating Meritax's analytics-driven approach and technology-enabled advocacy to keep pace with evolving property tax systems. Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, said the addition brings a disciplined, client-focused team with deep Texas knowledge to the firm.As part of the integration, Meritax partners including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with about 20 other team members, will join Ryan. The move adds to Ryan’s global footprint, which already serves more than 74,000 clients with a workforce of over 7,100 professionals, reinforcing the firm’s leading position in business taxes.

+ VETERANS DAY FEATURE: ANNA GRIFFIN CONNECTS MILITARY SERVICE TO CONSTRUCTION EXCELLENCE AT RYAN

Anna Griffin, director of construction for Ryan Companies US, sat down on the AZ Big Podcast to discuss how her military background informs leadership and resilience in the construction industry. The interview, recorded 5 days ago, comes as Veterans Day is observed in Arizona and highlights Griffin's trajectory from Air Force service to overseeing Southwest construction projects. Griffin recently returned from a seven-month deployment to Jordan as aircraft maintenance squadron commander for the 165th Airlift Wing, and she notes that the two fields share common ground: "Construction has so many parallels to my military service," she noted. "You’re still serving — just in a different way. You’re building something lasting, something that supports people and communities." At Ryan Companies, Griffin guides project management teams across healthcare, multifamily housing, and education. After nearly four years at the firm, her leadership helps navigate the complexity of construction environments, with a focus on integrity, accountability, and mission readiness, qualities that served her in the military and now guide civilian projects. The AZ Big Podcast, hosted by Michael Gossie and Amy Lindsey, is sponsored by Burch & Cracchiolo and aims to spotlight how strong leadership and collaboration drive success across industries in Arizona's business landscape. Griffin's episode is timely as the state honors its veterans and recognizes the valuable skills they contribute to the workforce. Listeners are encouraged to tune in to hear her compelling story and understand how veterans like her are making meaningful contributions to sectors such as construction. As she continues her work with Ryan Companies, her journey demonstrates the strength and adaptability of veterans transitioning into fresh roles, underscoring that the capabilities developed in military service are transferable across fields.

urn:contentItem:6JHK-KSM3-S0RF-V4BK-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Caterpillar EPS increases 7% Corporate Wire Date: 01 May 2026 12:35 EDT Release Date: April 30, 20262026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Caterpillar EPS increases 7% Corporate Wire Date: 01 May 2026 12:35 EDT Release Date: April 30, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT), announced EPS of $5.5 for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 6.8% sequentially from $5.15 in the previous quarter [Q4/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 27.2% from $2.0b to $2.5b

- Total revenue was up 22.2% from $14.2b to $17.4b

- EPS growth 30.3% from $4.22 to $5.5

- EBIT to Sales was up from 18% to 18.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 18% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 14.1% to 14.6%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was down from 4% to 3.8%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Dec 202530 Sep 2025EPS, $5.55.24.9Sequential growth in EPS %6.84.95.8Revenue, $ billion17.419.117.6Sequential growth in Revenue %-9.08.56.5Net Profit, $ billion2.52.42.3Sequential growth in Net Profit %6.14.45.6

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 30.3%, Revenue was up 22.2% and Net Profit was up 27.2%.

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, $5.54.2PCP growth in EPS %30-27Revenue, $ billion17.414.2PCP growth in Revenue %22.2-9.8Net Profit, $ billion2.52.0PCP growth in Net Profit %27.2-29.9

ACTIVITIES:

Caterpillar Inc., headquartered in Deerfield, Illinois, is a global leader in the manufacturing and sale of construction and mining equipment, diesel and natural gas engines, industrial turbines, and locomotives. Founded in 1925, the company operates through four primary segments: Construction Industries, Resource Industries, Energy & Transportation, and Financial Products, serving diverse markets including infrastructure, energy, and transportation. Its core activities encompass designing, producing, and distributing machinery for earthmoving, material handling, and power generation, while also offering aftermarket parts, services, and financing solutions to enhance customer productivity. Among its fastest-growing segments are Energy & Transportation, driven by rising demand for power systems in renewable energy projects and data centers, and digital solutions like Cat Connect, which leverage IoT for equipment monitoring and predictive maintenance. This strategic focus on innovation and sustainability positions Caterpillar to capitalize on global infrastructure investments and environmental regulations.

MARKET POSITION:

Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT), the NYSE's largest Industrial Machinery company by market cap, soared $320.17 (or 56.1%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $890.62. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 5.3% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 50.8%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $2,688 [vs $1,432 for the S&P 500 Index], for a capital gain of $1,615 and dividend reinvested of $72. The average annualised return to shareholders has been 63.9% in the past two years. The average daily volume of 1.8 million shares so far in 2026 was 0.7 times the average daily volume in 2025.

value.Title: SIU Carbondale to offer bachelor’s degree in AI+
value.Overview: The Southern Illinoisan (Carbondale, Illinois), NEWS, 901words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHK-FDX3-RVF9-445T-00000-00SIU Carbondale to offer bachelor’s degree in AI+2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisNEWSSIU Carbondale to offer bachelor’s degree in AI+BROOKE KELTNER For The Southern

CARBONDALE Beginning with the fall 2026 semester, Southern Illinois University Carbondale will become the first public university in Illinois to offer a Bachelor of Science degree in Artificial Intelligence Plus (AI+).

"Artificial intelligence technology is advancing rapidly," said Xiaoqing "Frank" Liu, dean of the College of Engineering, Computing, Technology, and Mathematics. "Many students are expressing that they want to learn this technology in a systematic way. This program will fill an educational need in Illinois, particularly SouthernIllinois."

SIU Carbondale will join a short list of fewer than a dozen universities nationwide to offer a bachelor's degree in artificial intelligence+ giving SIU a competitive edge in attracting students while offering affordable tuition, Liu said.

AI+ is focused on teaching students artificial intelligence technologies while selecting a minor area to apply the technology, offering a unique interdisciplinary approach.

For the past three years, SIU has been working on developing this program. School of Computing faculty and partnering programs across the SIU campus contributed to program development. The AI+ major builds upon SIU's computing curriculum, adding an emphasis on artificial intelligence and machine learning, according to Chun-Hsi "Vincent" Huang, the School of Computing director. Students will be required to take 39 credit hours of core university curriculum, nine hours of general electives, 49 credit hours of program foundation and required courses, and 12 credit hours of major electives related to artificial intelligence.

All students will also be required to complete a minimum 12-hour credit minor from our partnering programs. The program will allow students the choice of an area to apply artificial intelligence, currently including:

Art: Foundational courses in studio and art history. Ideal for students who are interested in design, visualization, and creative AI applications.

Business analytics: Emphasizes applying big data or new technologies such as AI in business settings.

Computer science: Build in-depth algorithms, data structures, and advanced AI-related computing.

Construction management and operations: Focuses on project planning, digital tools, and sustainability in applied engineering contexts.

Geographic information systems (GIS): Allows students to apply AI techniques in current geospatial techniques and analytical skills.

Information technologies: Emphasizes applied tech skills in networks, systems, and software relevant to AI.

Journalism: Focuses on media, writing, and communications, supporting ethical AI narratives and media technologies.

Marketing: Emphasizes effective marketing, which is essential to organizations in their efforts to achieve a competitive advantage that can be sustained.

Mathematics: Enhances theoretical foundations for AI through advanced courses in logic, modeling, and statistics.

Music: Explores sound, performance, and creativity, complementing work in audio AI and interactive media.

Sustainability: With AI techniques, students can further expand their knowledge and understanding of long-term sustainability of the earth's resources, including water, land use, food systems, climate change, urban sustainability, and green energy.

Theater: Offers experience in collaborative design, storytelling, and performance, relevant for human-centered AI design.

Liu believes the program stands out because of its interdisciplinary approach compared to traditional, highly structured computer science programs.

"AI+ focuses on the application of AI technology," Liu said. "Students will learn the artificial intelligence technology and get a minor in the area where they want to apply it. This is unique because not only will students take AI classes, but they also need to think about how to apply this technology to the public domain."

Serving students across campus

The program is open to all students on campus, not just those enrolled in the College of Engineering, Computing, Technology, and Mathematics, Huang said.

"Several courses in this program do not require too many prerequisites, so other majors can benefit from the course offerings," he said. "We also plan to offer some general education courses in artificial intelligence."

Additional information and admission requirements are available on the program's website.

The program's future steps include adding more minors, offering an online version, as well as creating an AI+ minor, certifications or a graduate degree.

Filling a need

Knowing AI techniques is becoming increasingly important in the job market, and the need for the skill set is expected to grow. Industries like healthcare, finance, manufacturing, marketing, education and logistics are moving toward this technology to optimize operations.

"Recent industry reports from the World Economic Forum and McKinsey Global Institute show the annual growth rate of jobs requiring AI skills is projected at 20-25% compound annual growth through 2030," Huang said.

He believes students majoring in AI will have a competitive edge when they graduate and enter the workforce as they can use their skill set to solve real-world problems.

"The training of artificial intelligence now requires more extensive exposure dedicated to AI," he said. "Students majoring in AI+ and with their selected minor will be strong candidates when it comes to hiring."

value.Title: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE
value.Overview: News Bites - Private Companies, STOCK, 1415words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-92P3-RWC8-P4G8-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CHICAGO BUILDER CLAYCO BETS ON SOLAR AND STORAGE WITH NEW NATIONAL ARM

Clayco, a Chicago-based contractor, is expanding into solar and storage with a new national division, announced on 4th March 2026. The venture, known as Clayco Power and Energy, aims to create nearly 1,000 jobs nationwide by 2027 and generate about $300 million in annual revenue.The unit will oversee design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and storage projects and is already advancing a 350-acre Manteno, Illinois site, with construction slated to begin this June.The expansion comes as U.S. electricity demand is forecast to rise 15%–20% by 2030, with solar and storage contributing a growing share of new capacity after accounting for roughly 85% of last year’s additions.

+ IDAHO NATIONAL LAB TO HOST NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER: CLAYCO TO LEAD DOE PROPOSAL DESIGN-BUILD EFFORT

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.In its advisory role, Clayco will align design and construction approaches with the operational requirements of high-density AI workloads, leveraging its integrated delivery model to meet the fast, reliable, and precise needs of modern data centers. The consortium brings together organizations with expertise in advanced nuclear technology, energy systems, and risk modeling, complementing Clayco's practical delivery capabilities. Deep Atomic's approach combines advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to ensure reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The collaboration aims to establish a scalable platform that could position the U.S. as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco helping integrate innovative energy solutions and execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ FOUNTAIN PARK AS BEACON OF RENEWAL: CLAYCO'S CLARK CALLS FOR COLLABORATIVE RECOVERY

Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark says the business community must join with city leaders to forge a unified rebuilding strategy for areas that faced long-standing struggles. In Fountain Park, he voiced cautious optimism about renewal and noted the visible damage from the disaster.Clark said Clayco acted promptly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Spencer, city services, and local partners. The company’s commitment to long-term rebuilding includes funding and staffing programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward, complemented by ongoing community support.Describing Fountain Park as a potential beacon of hope, Clark highlighted preexisting programs that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes, such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority. He emphasized that real recovery starts with the civic community and requires a shared will to move forward, adding that progress happens incrementally—one house at a time, one block at a time.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [2 News That Matters]

+ MERITAX ADVISORS JOINS RYAN COMPANIES TO ACCELERATE PROPERTY TAX SOLUTIONS

Ryan Companies expanded its property tax services with the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, announced official on the 10th of February 2026. Meritax, a Texas-based commercial property tax consultant, has supported clients across various real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value in Texas and beyond.The deal is designed to boost Ryan's capabilities in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services, while incorporating Meritax's analytics-driven approach and technology-enabled advocacy to keep pace with evolving property tax systems. Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, said the addition brings a disciplined, client-focused team with deep Texas knowledge to the firm.As part of the integration, Meritax partners including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with about 20 other team members, will join Ryan. The move adds to Ryan’s global footprint, which already serves more than 74,000 clients with a workforce of over 7,100 professionals, reinforcing the firm’s leading position in business taxes.

+ VETERANS DAY FEATURE: ANNA GRIFFIN CONNECTS MILITARY SERVICE TO CONSTRUCTION EXCELLENCE AT RYAN

Anna Griffin, director of construction for Ryan Companies US, sat down on the AZ Big Podcast to discuss how her military background informs leadership and resilience in the construction industry. The interview, recorded 5 days ago, comes as Veterans Day is observed in Arizona and highlights Griffin's trajectory from Air Force service to overseeing Southwest construction projects. Griffin recently returned from a seven-month deployment to Jordan as aircraft maintenance squadron commander for the 165th Airlift Wing, and she notes that the two fields share common ground: "Construction has so many parallels to my military service," she noted. "You’re still serving — just in a different way. You’re building something lasting, something that supports people and communities." At Ryan Companies, Griffin guides project management teams across healthcare, multifamily housing, and education. After nearly four years at the firm, her leadership helps navigate the complexity of construction environments, with a focus on integrity, accountability, and mission readiness, qualities that served her in the military and now guide civilian projects. The AZ Big Podcast, hosted by Michael Gossie and Amy Lindsey, is sponsored by Burch & Cracchiolo and aims to spotlight how strong leadership and collaboration drive success across industries in Arizona's business landscape. Griffin's episode is timely as the state honors its veterans and recognizes the valuable skills they contribute to the workforce. Listeners are encouraged to tune in to hear her compelling story and understand how veterans like her are making meaningful contributions to sectors such as construction. As she continues her work with Ryan Companies, her journey demonstrates the strength and adaptability of veterans transitioning into fresh roles, underscoring that the capabilities developed in military service are transferable across fields.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Green tax incentive boosts IL film industry
value.Overview: The Lincoln Courier (Illinois), NEWS; Pg. A1, 507words

urn:contentItem:6JJ1-J733-RTJ2-X45J-00000-00Green tax incentive boosts IL film industry2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisNEWSPg. A1Green tax incentive boosts IL film industryByJanis ReeserSpringfield State Journal-Register

Illinois film and television productions that meet green certification standards can now earn an extra 5% tax credit, making the state the first in the nation to offer such an incentive, according to a community announcement.

The initiative, launched in celebration of Earth Day, is expected to boost both the entertainment industry and the state's green economy, according to the announcement.

Productions recognized as a "certified green production" through the Illinois Film Office must meet environmental benchmarks such as reducing waste, lowering energy use and emissions, utilizing sustainable materials and implementing responsible food practices.

Illinois film industry

sees record growth

The announcement comes as Illinois' film industry continues to grow, with production expenditures reaching a record-breaking $703 million in 2025 and supporting an estimated 18,000 industry hires. The expansion of the tax credit is expected to drive demand for clean energy infrastructure, such as battery systems, solar-powered trailers and renewable energy solutions, creating new jobs across the state.

Chicago now ranks as the third-best big city for those who work in the film industry, according to the announcement.

Officials applaud Illinois film industry expansion

"This expansion reflects our commitment to growing Illinois' economy in a way that is both competitive and sustainable," Kristin Richards, director of the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity, said in the announcement. "We are attracting world-class productions while building the infrastructure and workforce needed for a cleaner future."

The new incentive is also expected to drive growth in Illinois' clean energy and green vendor sectors. As productions adapt to meet certification requirements, demand will increase for infrastructure such as battery systems, solar-powered trailers and renewable energy solutions - in turn creating new, good-paying jobs across the state.

"As someone who has worked for years to help make productions in Illinois more sustainable, I am absolutely thrilled that Governor Pritzker and the Illinois Film Office have made Illinois the first state in the country to offer productions a meaningful incentive to go green," Ellen White, CEO and founder of Ecofixr, an Illinois-based full-service sustainability services company, said in the announcement. "This is going to be a gamechanger for Illinois and will undoubtedly help Illinois attract more productions and the green jobs that go with them."

In addition to long-term infrastructure growth, more immediate changes on set are expected, such as transitioning from paper scripts to digital tablets, replacing single-use plastic water bottles with refill stations, and reducing reliance on diesel in favor of cleaner energy alternatives.

value.Title: Illinois boosts film industry with green tax incentive
value.Overview: The State Journal-Register (Springfield, IL), NEWS; Pg. A1, 507words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-NGW3-S8R9-X23G-00000-00Illinois boosts film industry with green tax incentive2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisNEWSPg. A1Illinois boosts film industry with green tax incentiveByJanis ReeserSpringfield State Journal-Register

Illinois film and television productions that meet green certification standards can now earn an extra 5% tax credit, making the state the first in the nation to offer such an incentive, according to a community announcement.

The initiative, launched in celebration of Earth Day, is expected to boost both the entertainment industry and the state's green economy, according to the announcement.

Productions recognized as a "certified green production" through the Illinois Film Office must meet environmental benchmarks such as reducing waste, lowering energy use and emissions, utilizing sustainable materials and implementing responsible food practices.

Illinois film industry sees record growth

The announcement comes as Illinois' film industry continues to grow, with production expenditures reaching a record-breaking $703 million in 2025 and supporting an estimated 18,000 industry hires. The expansion of the tax credit is expected to drive demand for clean energy infrastructure, such as battery systems, solar-powered trailers and renewable energy solutions, creating new jobs across the state.

Chicago now ranks as the third-best big city for those who work in the film industry, according to the announcement.

Officials applaud Illinois film industry expansion

"This expansion reflects our commitment to growing Illinois' economy in a way that is both competitive and sustainable," Kristin Richards, director of the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity, said in the announcement. "We are attracting world-class productions while building the infrastructure and workforce needed for a cleaner future."

The new incentive is also expected to drive growth in Illinois' clean energy and green vendor sectors. As productions adapt to meet certification requirements, demand will increase for infrastructure such as battery systems, solar-powered trailers and renewable energy solutions - in turn creating new, good-paying jobs across the state.

"As someone who has worked for years to help make productions in Illinois more sustainable, I am absolutely thrilled that Governor Pritzker and the Illinois Film Office have made Illinois the first state in the country to offer productions a meaningful incentive to go green," Ellen White, CEO and founder of Ecofixr, an Illinois-based full-service sustainability services company, said in the announcement. "This is going to be a gamechanger for Illinois and will undoubtedly help Illinois attract more productions and the green jobs that go with them."

In addition to long-term infrastructure growth, more immediate changes on set are expected, such as transitioning from paper scripts to digital tablets, replacing single-use plastic water bottles with refill stations, and reducing reliance on diesel in favor of cleaner energy alternatives.

This story was created with the assistance of Artificial Intelligence (AI). Journalists were involved in every step of the information gathering, review, editing and publishing process. Learn more at https://cm.usatoday.com/ethical-conduct/.

urn:contentItem:6JHB-9XG3-RSRS-S4Y5-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CHICAGO BUILDER CLAYCO BETS ON SOLAR AND STORAGE WITH NEW NATIONAL ARM

Clayco, a Chicago-based contractor, is expanding into solar and storage with a new national division, announced on 4th March 2026. The venture, known as Clayco Power and Energy, aims to create nearly 1,000 jobs nationwide by 2027 and generate about $300 million in annual revenue.The unit will oversee design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and storage projects and is already advancing a 350-acre Manteno, Illinois site, with construction slated to begin this June.The expansion comes as U.S. electricity demand is forecast to rise 15%–20% by 2030, with solar and storage contributing a growing share of new capacity after accounting for roughly 85% of last year’s additions.

+ IDAHO NATIONAL LAB TO HOST NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER: CLAYCO TO LEAD DOE PROPOSAL DESIGN-BUILD EFFORT

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.In its advisory role, Clayco will align design and construction approaches with the operational requirements of high-density AI workloads, leveraging its integrated delivery model to meet the fast, reliable, and precise needs of modern data centers. The consortium brings together organizations with expertise in advanced nuclear technology, energy systems, and risk modeling, complementing Clayco's practical delivery capabilities. Deep Atomic's approach combines advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to ensure reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The collaboration aims to establish a scalable platform that could position the U.S. as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco helping integrate innovative energy solutions and execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ FOUNTAIN PARK AS BEACON OF RENEWAL: CLAYCO'S CLARK CALLS FOR COLLABORATIVE RECOVERY

Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark says the business community must join with city leaders to forge a unified rebuilding strategy for areas that faced long-standing struggles. In Fountain Park, he voiced cautious optimism about renewal and noted the visible damage from the disaster.Clark said Clayco acted promptly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Spencer, city services, and local partners. The company’s commitment to long-term rebuilding includes funding and staffing programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward, complemented by ongoing community support.Describing Fountain Park as a potential beacon of hope, Clark highlighted preexisting programs that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes, such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority. He emphasized that real recovery starts with the civic community and requires a shared will to move forward, adding that progress happens incrementally—one house at a time, one block at a time.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [2 News That Matters]

+ MERITAX ADVISORS JOINS RYAN COMPANIES TO ACCELERATE PROPERTY TAX SOLUTIONS

Ryan Companies expanded its property tax services with the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, announced official on the 10th of February 2026. Meritax, a Texas-based commercial property tax consultant, has supported clients across various real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value in Texas and beyond.The deal is designed to boost Ryan's capabilities in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services, while incorporating Meritax's analytics-driven approach and technology-enabled advocacy to keep pace with evolving property tax systems. Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, said the addition brings a disciplined, client-focused team with deep Texas knowledge to the firm.As part of the integration, Meritax partners including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with about 20 other team members, will join Ryan. The move adds to Ryan’s global footprint, which already serves more than 74,000 clients with a workforce of over 7,100 professionals, reinforcing the firm’s leading position in business taxes.

+ VETERANS DAY FEATURE: ANNA GRIFFIN CONNECTS MILITARY SERVICE TO CONSTRUCTION EXCELLENCE AT RYAN

Anna Griffin, director of construction for Ryan Companies US, sat down on the AZ Big Podcast to discuss how her military background informs leadership and resilience in the construction industry. The interview, recorded 5 days ago, comes as Veterans Day is observed in Arizona and highlights Griffin's trajectory from Air Force service to overseeing Southwest construction projects. Griffin recently returned from a seven-month deployment to Jordan as aircraft maintenance squadron commander for the 165th Airlift Wing, and she notes that the two fields share common ground: "Construction has so many parallels to my military service," she noted. "You’re still serving — just in a different way. You’re building something lasting, something that supports people and communities." At Ryan Companies, Griffin guides project management teams across healthcare, multifamily housing, and education. After nearly four years at the firm, her leadership helps navigate the complexity of construction environments, with a focus on integrity, accountability, and mission readiness, qualities that served her in the military and now guide civilian projects. The AZ Big Podcast, hosted by Michael Gossie and Amy Lindsey, is sponsored by Burch & Cracchiolo and aims to spotlight how strong leadership and collaboration drive success across industries in Arizona's business landscape. Griffin's episode is timely as the state honors its veterans and recognizes the valuable skills they contribute to the workforce. Listeners are encouraged to tune in to hear her compelling story and understand how veterans like her are making meaningful contributions to sectors such as construction. As she continues her work with Ryan Companies, her journey demonstrates the strength and adaptability of veterans transitioning into fresh roles, underscoring that the capabilities developed in military service are transferable across fields.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
