value.Title: McDermott Will & Schulte expands Energy & Project Finance team with partners Sebastian Chan and Eli Humphries
value.Overview: Targeted News Service, 445words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH1-BNM3-RXV5-S2JS-00000-00McDermott Will & Schulte expands Energy & Project Finance team with partners Sebastian Chan and Eli Humphries2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisMcDermott Will & Schulte expands Energy & Project Finance team with partners Sebastian Chan and Eli HumphriesTargeted News ServiceCHICAGO, Illinois

[Category: BizLaw/Legal] -- McDermottWill and Emery, a law firm, issued the following news release:

* * *

McDermott Will & Schulte expands Energy & Project Finance team with partners Sebastian Chan and Eli Humphries

*

As growth in the energy and infrastructure sectors accelerates, global law firm McDermott Will & Schulte continues to invest in its market-leading Energy & Project Finance platform with the addition of partners Sebastian Chan and Eli Humphries, strengthening transactional and private equity capabilities. Sebastian joins from Kirkland & Ellis while Eli joins from Norton Rose Fulbright.

Sebastian and Eli bring deep experience on the debt side of project finance transactions and will help meet rising client demand from leading private equity sponsors, financial institutions, and the federal government. As the project finance market evolves, deals are becoming more complex across renewable energy, infrastructure, LNG, and battery storage, spanning construction and long-term debt financings, tax equity-related structures, and public-private partnership matters.

"Sebastian and Eli meaningfully enhance our team and underscore our commitment to building a go-to platform for high value energy financings," said Harris Siskind, global head of McDermott's Transactions Practice Group. "Their work enables us to deliver even greater value on some of the energy sector's most critical and time-sensitive matters, supporting clients across the full lifecycle of conventional and renewable energy, as well as infrastructure projects."

With Sebastian's and Eli's arrivals, McDermott's Energy & Project Finance Practice continues to scale its market-leading capabilities, advising sponsors, developers, lenders, and investors across the full lifecycle of conventional and renewable energy and infrastructure projects.

"I was drawn to McDermott's growth in the energy and infrastructure sectors and the opportunity to play a meaningful role in the expansion of its energy finance platform," said Sebastian. "The firm's integrated Transactions practice and focus on innovation make it an ideal environment to help clients navigate increasingly nuanced financing challenges."

"McDermott has built a truly collaborative and forward-looking Energy & Project Finance practice with real momentum," said Eli. "I am excited to join a team that pairs technical excellence with a strong client-first approach and is shaping the future of the energy market."

***

urn:contentItem:6JH5-NGW3-S8R9-X2SV-00000-00Illinois boosts film industry growth with new green tax incentive2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisNEWSPg. A1Illinois boosts film industry growth with new green tax incentiveByJanis ReeserSpringfield State Journal-Register

The announcement comes

as Illinois' film industry continues to grow, with production expenditures reaching a record-breaking $703 million in 2025 and supporting an estimated 18,000 industry hires.

Illinois film and television productions that meet green certification standards can now earn an extra 5% tax credit, making the state the first in the nation to offer such an incentive, according to a community announcement.

The initiative, launched in celebration of Earth Day, is expected to boost both the entertainment industry and the state's green economy, according to the announcement.

Productions recognized as a "certified green production" through the Illinois Film Office must meet environmental benchmarks such as reducing waste, lowering energy use and emissions, utilizing sustainable materials and implementing responsible food practices.

Illinois film industry sees record growth

The announcement comes as Illinois' film industry continues to grow, with production expenditures reaching a record-breaking $703 million in 2025 and supporting an estimated 18,000 industry hires. The expansion of the tax credit is expected to drive demand for clean energy infrastructure, such as battery systems, solar-powered trailers and renewable energy solutions, creating new jobs across the state.

Chicago now ranks as the third-best big city for those who work in the film industry, according to the announcement.

Officials applaud Illinois film industry expansion

"This expansion reflects our commitment to growing Illinois' economy in a way that is both competitive and sustainable," Kristin Richards, director of the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity, said in the announcement. "We are attracting world-class productions while building the infrastructure and workforce needed for a cleaner future."

The new incentive is also expected to drive growth in Illinois' clean energy and green vendor sectors. As productions adapt to meet certification requirements, demand will increase for infrastructure such as battery systems, solar-powered trailers and renewable energy solutions - in turn creating new, good-paying jobs across the state.

"As someone who has worked for years to help make productions in Illinois more sustainable, I am absolutely thrilled that Governor Pritzker and the Illinois Film Office have made Illinois the first state in the country to offer productions a meaningful incentive to go green," Ellen White, CEO and founder of Ecofixr, an Illinois-based full-service sustainability services company, said in the announcement. "This is going to be a gamechanger for Illinois and will undoubtedly help Illinois attract more productions and the green jobs that go with them."

In addition to long-term infrastructure growth, more immediate changes on set are expected, such as transitioning from paper scripts to digital tablets, replacing single-use plastic water bottles with refill stations, and reducing reliance on diesel in favor of cleaner energy alternatives.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 298 words

Thu Apr 16, 2026: Exelon CEO Projects 19 Gigawatt Load Increase for ComEd by 2030

Exelon Corporation's CEO has announced ambitious plans for its ComEd unit, projecting a 19 gigawatt increase in load across Illinois by 2030. This statement was made during a recent event on the 17th of April 2026, where the CEO emphasized the importance of meeting the growing energy demands of the state while navigating the complexities of the energy transition.

The projected increase in capacity comes amid a broader shift in the energy landscape, where Exelon Corporation is grappling with the challenges and opportunities presented by renewable energy sources. While the focus on clean energy remains crucial, the CEO hinted at a potential shift in strategy to accommodate rising demand, suggesting that traditional energy sources may play a more significant role in the coming years.

Exelon Corporation's commitment to expanding ComEd's infrastructure aligns with its long-term vision of sustainability and reliability. The company aims to enhance its operational efficiency while ensuring that the energy needs of its customers are met. As part of this strategy, the CEO highlighted the need for increased investment in both renewable and traditional energy sources to support the projected load growth.

The CEO's remarks come at a time when the energy sector is facing scrutiny regarding its environmental impact. Exelon Corporation has been recognized for its commitment to sustainability, holding a strong MSCI ESG score that reflects its environmental, social, and governance practices. However, the potential shift away from a purely clean energy focus may raise questions among investors about the company's long-term sustainability strategies.

As Exelon Corporation embarks on this ambitious path, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the company's progress in achieving the 19 gigawatt load increase. The energy landscape in Illinois is evolving, and the success of ComEd's expansion plans may serve as a bellwether for similar initiatives across the United States.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 342 words

Thu Apr 16, 2026: Exelon Corporation Advances Vehicle-to-Grid Technology in Virtual Power Plant Initiative

Exelon Corporation is making strides in the burgeoning field of vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology, showcasing the potential for electric vehicles to act as virtual power plants. This development comes in light of a recent report by the University of Delaware, which emphasizes the financial and environmental benefits of integrating electric vehicles into the energy grid. On 16th April 2026, the collaboration between these entities was put into the spotlight, revealing how this technology could help offset the higher costs associated with electric vehicles.

The findings suggest that grid electricity payments derived from V2G technology could provide a financial incentive for electric vehicle owners. By allowing their vehicles to discharge energy back into the grid during peak demand times, EV drivers can receive compensation that ultimately lowers their vehicle ownership costs. This aligns with Exelon's broader commitment to renewable energy initiatives and sustainable practices, reinforcing the company's position within the evolving energy landscape.

A demonstration event organized by Nuvve Holding Corp. at a Delmarva Power site illustrated the practical applications of vehicle-to-grid systems. The event showcased how electric-battery vehicles can support grid stability while simultaneously providing financial returns for their owners. As states and municipalities increasingly pursue greener energy solutions, the role of Exelon in facilitating such advancements is becoming increasingly significant.

Moreover, the recent approval of two new automotive standards by SAE International further paves the way for the adoption of V2G technology. These standards are designed to enhance interoperability among electric vehicles and the grid, making it easier for consumers to participate in demand response programs. Exelon's involvement in this regulatory landscape positions the company as a key player in the future of energy management.

The U.S. Department of Energy has also recognized the potential of vehicle-to-grid technology, announcing new partnerships that will channel up to $6.5 million in federal funding towards technical teams across the country. This federal support underscores the growing recognition of V2G as a viable solution for energy management, and Exelon Corporation's ongoing initiatives will undoubtedly play a pivotal role in shaping the future of the energy sector.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 376 words

Thu Apr 16, 2026: Exelon CEO Advocates for Regulatory Easing to Combat Rising Energy Costs

Exelon Corporation's CEO Calvin Butler emphasized the urgent need for regulatory changes to address the rising costs of electricity. Speaking at the Semafor World Economy conference in Washington, DC, Butler pointed out that the increasing demand for power, coupled with insufficient supply, is leading to higher energy bills for consumers. He stated, "It's supply and demand. We have inadequate supply to meet this rising demand, and until we address that issue, all of our bills are going to continue to go up." This statement was made on 16th April 2026.

Butler highlighted that easing permitting rules could significantly aid the federal government's efforts to enhance energy production. While the Trump administration has been advocating for this initiative, Butler noted that it is ultimately up to Congress to enact the necessary reforms. The call for regulatory changes aligns with broader discussions in the energy sector regarding the need for investment in new plants and technologies to meet the growing demand for electricity.

In a related discussion, Honeywell CEO Vimal Kapur echoed Butler's sentiments, stressing that the surge in energy demand is not limited to AI data centers; developing nations are also contributing to the phenomenon. Kapur advocated for a diverse approach to energy generation, stating that various sources—including nuclear, biofuels, and renewables—must be utilized effectively. He remarked, "Everything has a place to play," underscoring the importance of a comprehensive strategy in the energy landscape.

Exelon's renewed interest in the power-generation sector comes after its spin-off of Constellation Energy and its nuclear fleet in 2022. Butler expressed hope that by re-entering this market, Exelon could help alleviate some of the financial pressures on consumers driven by the rising demand from large data centers and other industrial clients. He outlined potential strategies, including measures to ensure that data centers do not unfairly transfer costs to other consumers.

As energy costs continue to escalate, the discussions led by Exelon Corporation's leadership may serve as a catalyst for broader industry reforms. Butler's emphasis on the need for regulatory adjustments could resonate with policymakers who are increasingly aware of the urgent challenges facing the energy sector. The dialogue around easing rules and investing in diverse energy solutions is likely to shape the future of energy production and pricing in the United States.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-S553-S89F-P3YX-00000-00Rexel USA Expands Reach with Revere Electric Supply Acquisition2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisRexel USA Expands Reach with Revere Electric Supply Acquisition

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

13 May 2026 09:32 EDT

Rexel USA has announced its agreement to acquire Revere Electric Supply, a prominent electrical distributor based in Mokena, Illinois. The transaction, finalized on 13th May 2026, marks a significant step in Rexel USA's strategy to enhance its regional presence by collaborating with top-tier, service-oriented companies known for their robust customer relationships and technical expertise.

Established in 1919, Revere Electric Supply has evolved from a small distributor into a trusted provider serving NorthernIllinois, Central Illinois, Southeast Wisconsin, and Northwest Indiana. With ten operational locations and a workforce of 375 employees, Revere is recognized for its specialization in industrial automation and its long-standing relationships with electrical contractors. The company's solid foundation in customer service and technical solutions has positioned it as a key player in the Midwest industrial market.

The Eiseman family has been instrumental in shaping Revere's culture and reputation over its century-long history. Current owners Tom Eiseman Sr. and Erik Eiseman have maintained a focus on investing in people and building long-term relationships. Erik Eiseman emphasized the importance of Revere's people and culture, stating that the shared values with Rexel would enable the company to continue flourishing under the new partnership.

Post-acquisition, Revere will retain its name and local identity, ensuring that its strong customer relationships and market presence remain intact. Rexel USA has committed to preserving Revere's culture and leadership team, with John Cady continuing as President while also taking on expanded responsibilities for Rexel's broader Midwest operations. Rexel's Midwest Region President, Hani Masoudi, will support this transition while overseeing other key areas.

Roger Little, CEO of Rexel USA, highlighted the acquisition as a strategic move to bolster their presence in a critical geographic area. He expressed confidence in Revere's capabilities and commitment to customers, emphasizing the potential for growth and innovation as both companies work together. With a robust leadership team and strong industrial automation expertise, Revere is poised for continued success as part of Rexel USA.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 REXEL ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Rexel Activities

Activities

Rexel is a leading global distributor of electrical, automation, and digital solutions, headquartered in France, with operations spanning over 30 countries. The company's core business activities center on supplying a wide range of products, including electrical wires, cables, lighting, HVAC systems, and automation technologies, primarily to professionals in the construction, industrial, and services sectors. It also provides value-added services such as technical consulting, project management, and digital tools to enhance efficiency for its customers. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy solutions and digital platforms, fueled by rising demand for sustainable technologies and smart building systems. This growth is supported by Rexel's strategic investments in e-commerce and energy management, positioning the company to capitalize on global trends toward electrification and decarbonization. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 10th largest Electronics company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JK7-7K23-S048-H20P-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CLAYCO UNVEILS POWER AND ENERGY UNIT AIMING FOR 1,000 JOBS BY 2027

Chicago-based contractor Clayco has formed a new division dedicated to large solar and storage projects across the country. Unveiled on 4th March 2026, the unit, Clayco Power and Energy, targets almost 1,000 U.S. jobs by 2027 and expects roughly $300 million in annual revenue.Clayco Power and Energy will provide design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and battery storage systems. It is currently developing a 350-acre solar-plus-storage project in Manteno, Illinois, about an hour south of Chicago, with construction due to start this June.The move aligns with a projected rise in electricity demand of 15%–20% by 2030, driven by data centers, manufacturing, and the shift to electrified transportation. Notably, last year saw solar plus storage accounting for the majority of new capacity, underscoring the market tailwinds behind solar deployment.

+ CLAYCO PARTNERS WITH DEEP ATOMIC ON U.S. NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER AT IDAHO NATIONAL LABORATORY

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.Deep Atomic’s approach blends advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to deliver reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The partnership aims to establish a scalable, sustainable platform that could position the United States as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco guiding execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ CLARK URGES UNIFIED ACTION TO REBUILD FOUNTAIN PARK AFTER MAY 16 TORNADO

ST. LOUIS, Mo. (First Alert 4) - Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark told business and civic leaders that rallying with political figures is essential to craft a unified reconstruction strategy for areas already under stress. Standing in Fountain Park, he said the recovery has been slow but the neighborhood shows potential for renewal.Clark said Clayco moved quickly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Cara Spencer and city services along with local partners. The company’s long-term rebuilding effort includes funding and staffing community programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward. He praised residents for supporting each other in the immediate aftermath in the absence of formal plans.Looking ahead, he described Fountain Park as a beacon of hope and noted developments that were already under way before the tornado, including city programs such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes. He stressed that everything starts with the civic community and a shared will to navigate recovery.Clark framed recovery as a gradual process, drawing on parallels to Hurricane Katrina and underscoring the need to start somewhere. He reiterated a vision of incremental progress: one house at a time, one block at a time, one brick at a time.Despite challenges, including more than 65% of affected property owners not applying for city programs, the city extended the deadline for its private property assistance program to February 28 to boost participation. Clark pointed to visible improvements since the tornado and cited the Delmar Divine development as evidence that teamwork can drive meaningful change.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [1 News That Matters]

+ RYAN TO BOLSTER PROPERTY TAX CAPABILITIES WITH MERITAX ADVISORS BUY

Ryan Companies has announced the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, a Texas-based commercial property tax consulting firm, with the move made official on the 10th of February 2026. The deal broadens Ryan's property tax capabilities for commercial real estate owners amid a more technology-driven landscape, emphasizing enhancements in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services.Meritax Advisors, founded in 1995, has earned a strong reputation for property tax representation across varied commercial real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value for institutional and private clients in Texas and beyond. The integration is designed to augment Ryan's existing property tax services by bringing deeper analytics, real-time intelligence and predictive insights to support technology-driven advocacy for clients.Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, stated, 'With Meritax Advisors joining Ryan, we’re adding a disciplined, client-focused team with deep knowledge of the Texas property tax landscape.' The firm notes that the acquisition will strengthen Ryan's ability to serve commercial real estate owners not only in Texas but also on a national scale, leveraging Ryan's global footprint and proprietary technology.As part of the integration, key Meritax leaders will join Ryan, including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with 20 additional team members. The combined organization underscores Ryan’s commitment to expanding its Property Tax practice in a competitive market and aligns with the broader goal of serving more than 74,000 clients worldwide with a 7,100-strong professional team.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJT-YG93-S384-J403-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - RYAN COMPANIES AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - RYAN COMPANIES AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CHICAGO BUILDER CLAYCO BETS ON SOLAR AND STORAGE WITH NEW NATIONAL ARM

Clayco, a Chicago-based contractor, is expanding into solar and storage with a new national division, announced on 4th March 2026. The venture, known as Clayco Power and Energy, aims to create nearly 1,000 jobs nationwide by 2027 and generate about $300 million in annual revenue.The unit will oversee design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and storage projects and is already advancing a 350-acre Manteno, Illinois site, with construction slated to begin this June.The expansion comes as U.S. electricity demand is forecast to rise 15%–20% by 2030, with solar and storage contributing a growing share of new capacity after accounting for roughly 85% of last year’s additions.

+ IDAHO NATIONAL LAB TO HOST NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER: CLAYCO TO LEAD DOE PROPOSAL DESIGN-BUILD EFFORT

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.In its advisory role, Clayco will align design and construction approaches with the operational requirements of high-density AI workloads, leveraging its integrated delivery model to meet the fast, reliable, and precise needs of modern data centers. The consortium brings together organizations with expertise in advanced nuclear technology, energy systems, and risk modeling, complementing Clayco's practical delivery capabilities. Deep Atomic's approach combines advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to ensure reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The collaboration aims to establish a scalable platform that could position the U.S. as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco helping integrate innovative energy solutions and execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ FOUNTAIN PARK AS BEACON OF RENEWAL: CLAYCO'S CLARK CALLS FOR COLLABORATIVE RECOVERY

Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark says the business community must join with city leaders to forge a unified rebuilding strategy for areas that faced long-standing struggles. In Fountain Park, he voiced cautious optimism about renewal and noted the visible damage from the disaster.Clark said Clayco acted promptly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Spencer, city services, and local partners. The company’s commitment to long-term rebuilding includes funding and staffing programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward, complemented by ongoing community support.Describing Fountain Park as a potential beacon of hope, Clark highlighted preexisting programs that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes, such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority. He emphasized that real recovery starts with the civic community and requires a shared will to move forward, adding that progress happens incrementally—one house at a time, one block at a time.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [2 News That Matters]

+ MERITAX ADVISORS JOINS RYAN COMPANIES TO ACCELERATE PROPERTY TAX SOLUTIONS

Ryan Companies expanded its property tax services with the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, announced official on the 10th of February 2026. Meritax, a Texas-based commercial property tax consultant, has supported clients across various real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value in Texas and beyond.The deal is designed to boost Ryan's capabilities in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services, while incorporating Meritax's analytics-driven approach and technology-enabled advocacy to keep pace with evolving property tax systems. Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, said the addition brings a disciplined, client-focused team with deep Texas knowledge to the firm.As part of the integration, Meritax partners including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with about 20 other team members, will join Ryan. The move adds to Ryan’s global footprint, which already serves more than 74,000 clients with a workforce of over 7,100 professionals, reinforcing the firm’s leading position in business taxes.

+ VETERANS DAY FEATURE: ANNA GRIFFIN CONNECTS MILITARY SERVICE TO CONSTRUCTION EXCELLENCE AT RYAN

Anna Griffin, director of construction for Ryan Companies US, sat down on the AZ Big Podcast to discuss how her military background informs leadership and resilience in the construction industry. The interview, recorded 5 days ago, comes as Veterans Day is observed in Arizona and highlights Griffin's trajectory from Air Force service to overseeing Southwest construction projects. Griffin recently returned from a seven-month deployment to Jordan as aircraft maintenance squadron commander for the 165th Airlift Wing, and she notes that the two fields share common ground: "Construction has so many parallels to my military service," she noted. "You’re still serving — just in a different way. You’re building something lasting, something that supports people and communities." At Ryan Companies, Griffin guides project management teams across healthcare, multifamily housing, and education. After nearly four years at the firm, her leadership helps navigate the complexity of construction environments, with a focus on integrity, accountability, and mission readiness, qualities that served her in the military and now guide civilian projects. The AZ Big Podcast, hosted by Michael Gossie and Amy Lindsey, is sponsored by Burch & Cracchiolo and aims to spotlight how strong leadership and collaboration drive success across industries in Arizona's business landscape. Griffin's episode is timely as the state honors its veterans and recognizes the valuable skills they contribute to the workforce. Listeners are encouraged to tune in to hear her compelling story and understand how veterans like her are making meaningful contributions to sectors such as construction. As she continues her work with Ryan Companies, her journey demonstrates the strength and adaptability of veterans transitioning into fresh roles, underscoring that the capabilities developed in military service are transferable across fields.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JK1-XSF3-SHSN-V3XY-00000-00Past Week: Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) down 6% in 2 weeks; -$2.76 [-5.9%]2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisPast Week: Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) down 6% in 2 weeks; -$2.76 [-5.9%]

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC $44.26), the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, extended its slide in the past week ended Wednesday, shedding an additional 76.0c (or 1.7%) to close at $44.26. The shares have tumbled $2.76 (or 5.9%) over the past two weeks. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 2,180.0 points (or 8.0%) in the two weeks, this represented a relative price change of -13.9%.

The price ranged between a high of $44.98 on Tuesday May 12 and a low of $44.19 on Monday May 11.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading utility company centered on electricity generation and distribution, supporting resilient infrastructure and sustainable energy investments through modernized smart grid solutions. Integrating advanced technologies, Exelon's ComEd electrification strategy aims to manage a 19 GW load increase by 2030 within Illinois by leveraging Exelon virtual power plant services and demand response capabilities. The company's innovation pipeline includes pioneering the Exelon V2G pilot program and distributed energy resources integration, enhancing both grid services and capacity planning. Strategic capital expenditure plans underscore Exelon’s competitive strengths in renewable energy integration and utility modernization efforts.

Bullish Turning Point

Williams % R

The stock is oversold according to the Williams % R indicator of -89.8, suggesting the price is close to its 14-day low. The Williams % R was previously -70.9.

Wednesday's price drop

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) down 1.6% after a second downgrade

CHICAGO, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - Exelon Corporation's (NASDAQ: EXC $44.26) stock price is down 1.6%. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 302.1 points (1%) in the day, the relative price change was -2.6%. There were 6,260,239 shares worth $277.1 million traded today; the volume was 0.9 times the average daily trading volume of 7.1 million shares.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Exelon Corporation is a prominent U.S.-based energy company listed on the Nasdaq, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas delivery. As one of the largest utilities in the nation, it operates through subsidiaries like Commonwealth Edison, PECO, and BGE, serving millions of customers across multiple states. The company's core activities encompass a diverse energy portfolio, including nuclear, fossil fuel, and renewable sources, with a strong emphasis on reliable and sustainable power supply. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Exelon is also advancing grid modernization and smart technology investments to enhance efficiency and integrate more renewables, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon future while maintaining operational excellence and financial stability. It is the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading energy provider strategically positioned to capitalize on the clean energy transition. With a robust Exelon growth strategy, the company focuses on Exelon infrastructure investment and Exelon grid modernization, bolstering its status as a key player in power generation. Exelon's comprehensive Exelon capital expenditures and Exelon capex plans underscore its commitment to modernizing its Exelon nuclear fleet and advancing Exelon transmission projects. The firm’s Exelon capital allocation prioritizes Exelon renewable energy projects and Exelon distribution upgrades, enhancing grid reliability. Recent ventures, including Exelon Constellation acquisition, fortify its market position. Exelon's clean energy transition aligns with its sustainability goals and Exelon utility services contribute significantly to earnings guidance. Analyst ratings on Exelon (EXC) stock reflect confidence in Exelon stock outlook and Exelon dividend outlook. As EXC quarterly results continue to meet expectations, Exelon long-term targets remain focused on infrastructure capex and modernization plans.

urn:contentItem:6JK2-3F43-SHSP-W0HS-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - UPDATES FROM RYAN COMPANIES AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - UPDATES FROM RYAN COMPANIES AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CHICAGO BUILDER CLAYCO BETS ON SOLAR AND STORAGE WITH NEW NATIONAL ARM

Clayco, a Chicago-based contractor, is expanding into solar and storage with a new national division, announced on 4th March 2026. The venture, known as Clayco Power and Energy, aims to create nearly 1,000 jobs nationwide by 2027 and generate about $300 million in annual revenue.The unit will oversee design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and storage projects and is already advancing a 350-acre Manteno, Illinois site, with construction slated to begin this June.The expansion comes as U.S. electricity demand is forecast to rise 15%–20% by 2030, with solar and storage contributing a growing share of new capacity after accounting for roughly 85% of last year’s additions.

+ IDAHO NATIONAL LAB TO HOST NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER: CLAYCO TO LEAD DOE PROPOSAL DESIGN-BUILD EFFORT

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.In its advisory role, Clayco will align design and construction approaches with the operational requirements of high-density AI workloads, leveraging its integrated delivery model to meet the fast, reliable, and precise needs of modern data centers. The consortium brings together organizations with expertise in advanced nuclear technology, energy systems, and risk modeling, complementing Clayco's practical delivery capabilities. Deep Atomic's approach combines advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to ensure reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The collaboration aims to establish a scalable platform that could position the U.S. as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco helping integrate innovative energy solutions and execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ FOUNTAIN PARK AS BEACON OF RENEWAL: CLAYCO'S CLARK CALLS FOR COLLABORATIVE RECOVERY

Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark says the business community must join with city leaders to forge a unified rebuilding strategy for areas that faced long-standing struggles. In Fountain Park, he voiced cautious optimism about renewal and noted the visible damage from the disaster.Clark said Clayco acted promptly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Spencer, city services, and local partners. The company’s commitment to long-term rebuilding includes funding and staffing programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward, complemented by ongoing community support.Describing Fountain Park as a potential beacon of hope, Clark highlighted preexisting programs that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes, such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority. He emphasized that real recovery starts with the civic community and requires a shared will to move forward, adding that progress happens incrementally—one house at a time, one block at a time.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [1 News That Matters]

+ MERITAX ADVISORS JOINS RYAN COMPANIES TO ACCELERATE PROPERTY TAX SOLUTIONS

Ryan Companies expanded its property tax services with the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, announced official on the 10th of February 2026. Meritax, a Texas-based commercial property tax consultant, has supported clients across various real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value in Texas and beyond.The deal is designed to boost Ryan's capabilities in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services, while incorporating Meritax's analytics-driven approach and technology-enabled advocacy to keep pace with evolving property tax systems. Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, said the addition brings a disciplined, client-focused team with deep Texas knowledge to the firm.As part of the integration, Meritax partners including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with about 20 other team members, will join Ryan. The move adds to Ryan’s global footprint, which already serves more than 74,000 clients with a workforce of over 7,100 professionals, reinforcing the firm’s leading position in business taxes.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC $44.98), the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, extended its slide in the past week ended Tuesday, shedding an additional $1.20 (or 2.6%) to close at $44.98. The shares have sunk $2.06 (or 4.4%) over the past two weeks. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 2,035.8 points (or 7.5%) in the two weeks, this represented a relative price change of -11.9%.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 6.9% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 4.0%.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading utility company centered on electricity generation and distribution, supporting resilient infrastructure and sustainable energy investments through modernized smart grid solutions. Integrating advanced technologies, Exelon's ComEd electrification strategy aims to manage a 19 GW load increase by 2030 within Illinois by leveraging Exelon virtual power plant services and demand response capabilities. The company's innovation pipeline includes pioneering the Exelon V2G pilot program and distributed energy resources integration, enhancing both grid services and capacity planning. Strategic capital expenditure plans underscore Exelon’s competitive strengths in renewable energy integration and utility modernization efforts.

Tuesday's price up

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) inches higher, resistance at 12-day EMA

CHICAGO, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Exelon Corporation's (NASDAQ: EXC $44.96) stock price lifted 1.7% but fell short of the 12-day Exponential Moving Average [EMA] of $45.42. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which fell 255.9 points (0.9%) in the day, the relative price increase was 2.6%. The stock last traded at $44.96. Today its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 2.5% was 1.4 times its average daily volatility of 1.8%. Today the stock traded at its six-day high of $45.13. The low for the day was $44.05. There were 6,157,631 shares worth $276.8 million traded today; the volume was 0.9 times the average daily trading volume of 7.1 million shares.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Exelon Corporation is a prominent U.S.-based energy company listed on the Nasdaq, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas delivery. As one of the largest utilities in the nation, it operates through subsidiaries like Commonwealth Edison, PECO, and BGE, serving millions of customers across multiple states. The company's core activities encompass a diverse energy portfolio, including nuclear, fossil fuel, and renewable sources, with a strong emphasis on reliable and sustainable power supply. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Exelon is also advancing grid modernization and smart technology investments to enhance efficiency and integrate more renewables, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon future while maintaining operational excellence and financial stability. It is the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading energy provider strategically positioned to capitalize on the clean energy transition. With a robust Exelon growth strategy, the company focuses on Exelon infrastructure investment and Exelon grid modernization, bolstering its status as a key player in power generation. Exelon's comprehensive Exelon capital expenditures and Exelon capex plans underscore its commitment to modernizing its Exelon nuclear fleet and advancing Exelon transmission projects. The firm’s Exelon capital allocation prioritizes Exelon renewable energy projects and Exelon distribution upgrades, enhancing grid reliability. Recent ventures, including Exelon Constellation acquisition, fortify its market position. Exelon's clean energy transition aligns with its sustainability goals and Exelon utility services contribute significantly to earnings guidance. Analyst ratings on Exelon (EXC) stock reflect confidence in Exelon stock outlook and Exelon dividend outlook. As EXC quarterly results continue to meet expectations, Exelon long-term targets remain focused on infrastructure capex and modernization plans.

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC $43.91), the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, has fell $2.59 (or 5.6%) in the past week to close at $43.91.

This makes it the sixth lowest performer in the NASDAQ-100 Index which was up 5.5% in the past week; a relative price change of -11.1%.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 6.9% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 4.1%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $2.6 billion to $44.3 billion.

The volume was 1.2 times average trading per week of 35.7 million shares.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading utility company centered on electricity generation and distribution, supporting resilient infrastructure and sustainable energy investments through modernized smart grid solutions. Integrating advanced technologies, Exelon's ComEd electrification strategy aims to manage a 19 GW load increase by 2030 within Illinois by leveraging Exelon virtual power plant services and demand response capabilities. The company's innovation pipeline includes pioneering the Exelon V2G pilot program and distributed energy resources integration, enhancing both grid services and capacity planning. Strategic capital expenditure plans underscore Exelon’s competitive strengths in renewable energy integration and utility modernization efforts.

Financial News

Annual Report (FY 2025):

Release Date: February 13, 2026

FY2025 Annual Report: Exelon Net Profit up 13%

Exelon (NASDAQ:EXC) reported net profit for the year-ended 31 December 2025 [FY2025] of $2.8b, up 13% from $2.5b in the previous year [FY2024]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 12% from $2.45 in FY2024 to $2.74 in FY2025.

Annual growth in Revenue, Net Profit and EPS

Friday's price drop

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) in 4th consecutive fall

CHICAGO, Friday, May 08 2026 - Exelon Corporation's (NASDAQ: EXC $43.91) stock price slipped further on Friday, sliding an additional 50.0c (1.1%) to close at $43.91. There were 5,422,813 shares worth $238.1 million traded today.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Exelon Corporation is a prominent U.S.-based energy company listed on the Nasdaq, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas delivery. As one of the largest utilities in the nation, it operates through subsidiaries like Commonwealth Edison, PECO, and BGE, serving millions of customers across multiple states. The company's core activities encompass a diverse energy portfolio, including nuclear, fossil fuel, and renewable sources, with a strong emphasis on reliable and sustainable power supply. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Exelon is also advancing grid modernization and smart technology investments to enhance efficiency and integrate more renewables, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon future while maintaining operational excellence and financial stability. It is the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading energy provider strategically positioned to capitalize on the clean energy transition. With a robust Exelon growth strategy, the company focuses on Exelon infrastructure investment and Exelon grid modernization, bolstering its status as a key player in power generation. Exelon's comprehensive Exelon capital expenditures and Exelon capex plans underscore its commitment to modernizing its Exelon nuclear fleet and advancing Exelon transmission projects. The firm’s Exelon capital allocation prioritizes Exelon renewable energy projects and Exelon distribution upgrades, enhancing grid reliability. Recent ventures, including Exelon Constellation acquisition, fortify its market position. Exelon's clean energy transition aligns with its sustainability goals and Exelon utility services contribute significantly to earnings guidance. Analyst ratings on Exelon (EXC) stock reflect confidence in Exelon stock outlook and Exelon dividend outlook. As EXC quarterly results continue to meet expectations, Exelon long-term targets remain focused on infrastructure capex and modernization plans.

urn:contentItem:6JJD-GYS3-S6SD-R560-00000-00Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) falls 5.6% in Week 18 of 2026: Latest News and Company Communication2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisExelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) falls 5.6% in Week 18 of 2026: Latest News and Company Communication

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC $43.91), the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, has fell $2.59 (or 5.6%) in the past week to close at $43.91.

This makes it the sixth lowest performer in the NASDAQ-100 Index which was up 5.5% in the past week; a relative price change of -11.1%.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 6.9% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 4.1%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $2.6 billion to $44.3 billion.

The volume was 1.2 times average trading per week of 35.7 million shares.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading utility company centered on electricity generation and distribution, supporting resilient infrastructure and sustainable energy investments through modernized smart grid solutions. Integrating advanced technologies, Exelon's ComEd electrification strategy aims to manage a 19 GW load increase by 2030 within Illinois by leveraging Exelon virtual power plant services and demand response capabilities. The company's innovation pipeline includes pioneering the Exelon V2G pilot program and distributed energy resources integration, enhancing both grid services and capacity planning. Strategic capital expenditure plans underscore Exelon’s competitive strengths in renewable energy integration and utility modernization efforts.

Annual Report (FY 2025):

Release Date: February 13, 2026

FY2025 Annual Report: Exelon Net Profit up 13%

Exelon (NASDAQ:EXC) reported net profit for the year-ended 31 December 2025 [FY2025] of $2.8b, up 13% from $2.5b in the previous year [FY2024]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 12% from $2.45 in FY2024 to $2.74 in FY2025.

Friday's price drop

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) in 4th consecutive fall

CHICAGO, Friday, May 08 2026 - Exelon Corporation's (NASDAQ: EXC $43.91) stock price slipped further on Friday, sliding an additional 50.0c (1.1%) to close at $43.91. There were 5,422,813 shares worth $238.1 million traded today.

Exelon is a utility services holding company engaged in the energy transmission and distribution businesses through its six reportable segments: ComEd, PECO, BGE, Pepco, DPL, and ACE. August 26 2025: EA SPORTS FC 26 revealed as our most community-driven game yet

This summer, EA proudly unveiled EA SPORTS FC 26-launching worldwide on September 26, 2025 for PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, Xbox Series X|S, Xbox One, PC, Amazon Luna, Nintendo Switch(TradeMark), and Nintendo Switch(TradeMark) 2. The reveal event took place over two action-packed days at Electronic Arts Vancouver, where creators and journalists from around the world were invited to discover every new detail of the game, tour our world-class motion capture studio, chat one-on-one with developers, and get hands-on with the latest build.

The excitement in the air made it clear-this year's reveal was more than just a preview; it was a true celebration of the beautiful game.

All of this momentum is building toward our most community-driven FC ever, with groundbreaking new features inspired by our fans and brought to life by our world-class development team.

July 31 2025: Exelon Reports Second Quarter 2025 Results

Earnings Release Highlights GAAP net income of $0.39 per share and Adjusted (non-GAAP) operating earnings of $0.39 per share for the second quarter of 2025 Affirming full year 2025 Adjusted (non-GAAP) operating earnings guidance range of $2.64-$2.74 per share

Reaffirming operating EPS compounded annual growth of 5-7% from 2024 to 2028

All utilities sustained top quartile or better performance in reliability and BGE, PECO, and PHI sustained top quartile or better performance in gas odor response

Executed ~80% of planned debt financings and continued strong progress on equity financing, having now priced 100% of $700 million annualized equity financing need for 2025 and ~22% for 2026

Chicago, IL - Exelon Corporation (Nasdaq: EXC) today reported its financial results for the second quarter of 2025.

"Exelon's second-quarter performance reflects our disciplined execution across all fronts," said Exelon President and Chief Executive Officer Calvin Butler. "We remain focused on delivering long-term value through operational excellence, customer affordability solutions and a balanced investment strategy that supports grid modernization and energy security. As we reaffirm our financial guidance, we are confident in our ability to meet the evolving needs of our customers and communities while advancing a cleaner, more resilient energy future."

"I'm pleased to announce we delivered second quarter 2025 adjusted operating earnings of $0.39 per share, overcoming an active start to the summer storm season, including one of the largest in recent history at PECO with peak outages over 325,000 customers," said Exelon Chief Financial Officer Jeanne Jones. "We remain on track to deliver within our full-year earnings guidance range of $2.64 - $2.74 per share, and our performance underscores our ability to deliver strong financial and operational results while keeping our customers front and center."

Second Quarter 2025

Exelon's GAAP net income for the second quarter of 2025 decreased to $0.39 per share from $0.45 per share in the second quarter of 2024.

Press Release article 8 of 12, Source: Company Website, 288 words

July 28 2025: Exelixis Announces Second Quarter 2025 Financial Results and Provides Corporate Update

Total Revenues of $568.3 million, Cabozantinib Franchise U.S. Net Product Revenues of $520.0 million -

GAAP Diluted EPS of $0.65, Non-GAAP Diluted EPS of $0.75 -

Conference Call and Webcast Today at 5:00 PM Eastern Time -

ALAMEDA, Calif.---Jul. 28, 2025-- Exelixis, Inc. (Nasdaq: EXEL) today reported financial results for the second quarter of 2025, provided an update on progress toward achieving key corporate objectives, and outlined its commercial, clinical and pipeline development milestones.

"Exelixis continued to execute on our corporate objectives in the second quarter of 2025, delivering on key commercial, development and pipeline milestones," said Michael M. Morrissey, Ph.D., President and Chief Executive Officer, Exelixis. "While early in the launch, we're very pleased with the reception that CABOMETYX® has received in advanced neuroendocrine tumors (NET). Our commercial team rapidly mobilized on the U.S. NET launch following approval in March, capturing a leading share of new patient starts among oral therapies in second-line and later settings with NET representing approximately four percent of our overall CABOMETYX business in the second quarter. We will continue to track the launch trajectory and provide updates to our 2025 full year financial guidance, as appropriate."

Dr. Morrissey continued: "Turning to the zanzalintinib development program, in June, we announced positive topline results from the STELLAR-303 pivotal study in colorectal cancer. We plan to discuss these results with regulators with the intention of filing for approval in this indication as quickly as possible. In May, we completed enrollment into the STELLAR-304 pivotal study in non-clear cell renal cell carcinoma with top-line results expected in the first half of 2026, depending on study event rates.

Press Release article 9 of 12, Source: Company Website, 331 words

July 24 2025: Exelixis' Partner Ipsen Receives European Commission Approval for CABOMETYX® (cabozantinib) for Patients with Previously Treated Advanced Neuroendocrine Tumors

Exelixis, Inc. (Nasdaq: EXEL) today announced that its partner Ipsen received approval from the European Commission (EC) for CABOMETYX® (cabozantinib) for the treatment of adult patients with unresectable or metastatic, well-differentiated pancreatic (pNET) and extra-pancreatic (epNET) neuroendocrine tumors who have progressed following at least one prior systemic therapy other than somatostatin analogues. This approval follows the positive opinion received from the European Medicines Agency's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use in June 2025 and allows for the marketing of CABOMETYX in this indication in all 27 member states of the European Union (EU), Norway, Liechtenstein and Iceland.

"The availability of CABOMETYX in the European Union for patients with previously treated advanced neuroendocrine tumors is a significant milestone as there have been limited treatment advancements in the past decade, including very few options shown to improve outcomes across a heterogenous population," said Amy Peterson, M.D., Executive Vice President, Product Development and Medical Affairs, and Chief Medical Officer, Exelixis. "This approval builds on the global CABOMETYX franchise and extends its benefit to even more patients in need of new treatment options. We are proud to partner with Ipsen in our shared commitment to improving the standard of care for people living with advanced, difficult-to-treat cancers."

The EC approval is based on results from the phase 3 CABINET pivotal trial, which evaluated CABOMETYX compared with placebo in two cohorts of patients with previously treated NET: advanced pNET and advanced epNET. CABINET was the basis for the U.S. Food and Drug Administration approval of CABOMETYX in March 2025 for the treatment of 1) adult and pediatric patients 12 years of age and older with previously treated, unresectable, locally advanced or metastatic, well-differentiated pNET; and 2) adult and pediatric patients 12 years of age and older with previously treated, unresectable, locally advanced or metastatic, well-differentiated epNET.

Press Release article 10 of 12, Source: Company Website, 331 words

July 24 2025: Exelixis' Partner Ipsen Receives European Commission Approval for CABOMETYX® (cabozantinib) for Patients with Previously Treated Advanced Neuroendocrine Tumors

Exelixis, Inc. (Nasdaq: EXEL) today announced that its partner Ipsen received approval from the European Commission (EC) for CABOMETYX® (cabozantinib) for the treatment of adult patients with unresectable or metastatic, well-differentiated pancreatic (pNET) and extra-pancreatic (epNET) neuroendocrine tumors who have progressed following at least one prior systemic therapy other than somatostatin analogues. This approval follows the positive opinion received from the European Medicines Agency's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use in June 2025 and allows for the marketing of CABOMETYX in this indication in all 27 member states of the European Union (EU), Norway, Liechtenstein and Iceland.

"The availability of CABOMETYX in the European Union for patients with previously treated advanced neuroendocrine tumors is a significant milestone as there have been limited treatment advancements in the past decade, including very few options shown to improve outcomes across a heterogenous population," said Amy Peterson, M.D., Executive Vice President, Product Development and Medical Affairs, and Chief Medical Officer, Exelixis. "This approval builds on the global CABOMETYX franchise and extends its benefit to even more patients in need of new treatment options. We are proud to partner with Ipsen in our shared commitment to improving the standard of care for people living with advanced, difficult-to-treat cancers."

CABINET was the basis for the U.S. Food and Drug Administration approval of CABOMETYX in March 2025 for the treatment of 1) adult and pediatric patients 12 years of age and older with previously treated, unresectable, locally advanced or metastatic, well-differentiated pNET; and 2) adult and pediatric patients 12 years of age and older with previously treated, unresectable, locally advanced or metastatic, well-differentiated epNET.

July 14 2025: Exelixis to Release Second Quarter 2025 Financial Results on Monday, July 28, 2025

Exelixis, Inc. (Nasdaq: EXEL) announced today that its second quarter 2025 financial results will be released on Monday, July 28, 2025 after the markets close. At 5:00 p.m. ET / 2:00 p.m. PT, Exelixis management will host a conference call and webcast to discuss the results and provide a general business update. Access to the event is available via the Internet from the company's website.

To access the conference call, please register using this link. Upon registration, a dial-in number and unique PIN will be provided to join the call. To access the live webcast link, log onto www.exelixis.com and proceed to the Event Calendar page under the Investors & News heading. A webcast replay of the conference call will also be archived on www.exelixis.com for one year.

July 14 2025: Exelixis to Release Second Quarter 2025 Financial Results on Monday, July 28, 2025

Exelixis, Inc. (Nasdaq: EXEL) announced today that its second quarter 2025 financial results will be released on Monday, July 28, 2025 after the markets close. At 5:00 p.m. ET / 2:00 p.m. PT, Exelixis management will host a conference call and webcast to discuss the results and provide a general business update. Access to the event is available via the Internet from the company's website.

To access the conference call, please register using this link. Upon registration, a dial-in number and unique PIN will be provided to join the call. To access the live webcast link, log onto www.exelixis.com and proceed to the Event Calendar page under the Investors & News heading. A webcast replay of the conference call will also be archived on www.exelixis.com for one year.

Source : Company Website

May 07 2026: Exelon Corporation's Q1 2026 Performance Reflects Strategic Pivot

Exelon Corporation's Q1 2026 earnings call revealed a solid performance driven by favorable weather and strategic timing decisions. On the 7th of May 2026, the management team emphasized that these factors have allowed the company to stay on track with its full-year commitments, thus demonstrating resilience in a challenging operational environment.

One significant move discussed was the withdrawal of PECO's electric and gas rate cases in Pennsylvania, a decision made to address customer affordability concerns and stakeholder feedback. This decision reflects Exelon Corporation's commitment to balancing infrastructure needs with the interests of its customers, particularly in a regulatory climate that is becoming increasingly complex.

The company is also pivoting its capital plan by deferring $1.1 billion in distribution projects, while simultaneously adding $1.5 billion in transmission investment. This shift is aimed at supporting data center interconnections, a growing demand driven by the structural imbalance between energy supply and demand in the Mid-Atlantic region, which has seen supply prices rise by up to 80% over the last five years.

Looking ahead, Exelon Corporation reaffirmed its 2026 operating earnings guidance of $2.81 to $2.91 per share, with long-term growth expectations positioned at the higher end of the 5% to 7% range through 2029. The company's transmission rate base is projected to grow at an impressive rate of 16% annually, supported by a substantial opportunity pipeline valued between $12 billion and $17 billion outside its base plan.

However, the company faces challenges, including a potential credit downgrade for PECO following the recent rate case withdrawal and the implications of the Maryland Utility Relief Act. Management has cautioned that without new supply, customers in the PJM region may experience significant outage risks and continued price volatility by the 2028/2029 planning year. Amid these challenges, the transition of PECO's leadership to interim CEO Michael A. Innocenzo aims to ensure stability and leverage regional relationships during this sensitive time.

Google Streaming News 2 of 3, Source: marketscreener.com

May 06 2026: Exelon Corporation Sees Revenue Growth Amid Stable Earnings

Exelon Corporation has reported its earnings results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026. The company recorded sales of USD 7,242 million, showing an increase from USD 6,714 million in the same quarter of the previous year. This growth in revenue reflects Exelon’s ongoing efforts to enhance its operations and adapt to changing market conditions.

Net income for Exelon Corporation stood at USD 919 million, a slight rise from USD 908 million a year ago. Basic and diluted earnings per share from continuing operations remained stable at USD 0.9, unchanged from the first quarter of 2025. This consistency in earnings per share demonstrates Exelon’s ability to maintain profitability despite fluctuations in market dynamics.

CEO of Exelon Corporation highlighted a notable shift away from clean energy initiatives in the current economic landscape. This change may influence the company’s future strategies as it navigates the complexities of the energy market while striving to meet investor expectations. The company continues to focus on strengthening its fundamentals and optimizing its valuation metrics.

Exelon Corporation has received various composite ratings that assess its performance across multiple dimensions, including fundamentals, valuation, and financial health. These ratings suggest a robust framework for evaluating the company's operational effectiveness and long-term viability. Investors are encouraged to review these composite ratings carefully to gain insights into Exelon’s standing relative to its industry peers.

Additionally, Exelon Corporation's MSCI ESG score reflects its commitment to environmental, social, and governance practices, positioning it competitively within the industry. This score aids investors in factoring sustainability into their investment decisions. As Exelon continues to adapt to market changes, its focus on both financial performance and ESG practices will be crucial in attracting and retaining investor interest.

Google Streaming News 3 of 3, Source: Reuters

May 06 2026: Exelon Reports Strong Q1 Performance, Increases Capex Amid Data Center Demand

Exelon Corporation, a leading U.S. utility, has raised its capital expenditure plan and exceeded Wall Street's earnings expectations for the first quarter, thanks to a combination of higher electricity prices, robust demand, and favorable weather conditions. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026, as the company reported an adjusted profit of 91 cents per share, surpassing analysts' average estimate of 89 cents.

In light of increasing power demand driven by the proliferation of data centers supporting artificial intelligence tasks, Exelon has adjusted its projected capital expenditures for the next four years to $41.7 billion, up from a previous estimate of $41.3 billion. This strategic move aligns with the broader trend among U.S. utilities, which are ramping up their budgets to accommodate the growing energy needs of tech giants.

During a post-earnings conference call, Exelon executives revealed that the company, in partnership with Invenergy, has submitted bids for two significant transmission projects in Illinois, worth approximately $1.9 billion, as part of the regional grid operator MISO's Tranche 2.1 process. Additionally, Exelon's data-center pipeline benefits from Federal Energy Regulatory Commission-approved transmission security agreements, which include around $1 billion in collateral.

As part of its growth strategy, Exelon expects the value of its transmission assets for rate-setting purposes to rise by 16% through 2029, while its overall regulated asset base is anticipated to increase by 7.9%. Regulated utilities like Exelon utilize rate-case processes to determine pricing for electricity, natural gas, and additional services such as private water and steam.

In terms of financial performance, Exelon's Commonwealth Edison unit (ComEd), the largest electric utility in Illinois, reported a slight increase in net income to $310 million. Meanwhile, earnings at its PECO unit, Pennsylvania's largest electric and natural gas utility, rose by 4.5% to reach $278 million. Overall, Exelon generated revenue of $7.24 billion for the quarter ending on March 31st, surpassing analysts' average estimate of $6.93 billion, as compiled by LSEG.

SECTION 4 CORPORATE PROFILE

4.1 Activities

Exelon Corporation is a prominent U.S.-based energy company listed on the Nasdaq, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas delivery. As one of the largest utilities in the nation, it operates through subsidiaries like Commonwealth Edison, PECO, and BGE, serving millions of customers across multiple states. The company's core activities encompass a diverse energy portfolio, including nuclear, fossil fuel, and renewable sources, with a strong emphasis on reliable and sustainable power supply. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Exelon is also advancing grid modernization and smart technology investments to enhance efficiency and integrate more renewables, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon future while maintaining operational excellence and financial stability. It is the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading energy provider strategically positioned to capitalize on the clean energy transition. With a robust Exelon growth strategy, the company focuses on Exelon infrastructure investment and Exelon grid modernization, bolstering its status as a key player in power generation. Exelon's comprehensive Exelon capital expenditures and Exelon capex plans underscore its commitment to modernizing its Exelon nuclear fleet and advancing Exelon transmission projects. The firm’s Exelon capital allocation prioritizes Exelon renewable energy projects and Exelon distribution upgrades, enhancing grid reliability. Recent ventures, including Exelon Constellation acquisition, fortify its market position. Exelon's clean energy transition aligns with its sustainability goals and Exelon utility services contribute significantly to earnings guidance. Analyst ratings on Exelon (EXC) stock reflect confidence in Exelon stock outlook and Exelon dividend outlook. As EXC quarterly results continue to meet expectations, Exelon long-term targets remain focused on infrastructure capex and modernization plans.

AMERICAN RESULTS

Littelfuse (NASDAQ:LFUS) announced net profit for the quarter-ended 28 March 2026 [Q1/2026] of $75.1m, recovered from $242m in the previous quarter [Q4/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 28, 2026 vs Mar 29, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 72.5% from $43.6m to $75.1m

- Total revenue was up 17.1% from $559.1m to $654.6m

- EPS growth 70.5% from $1.76 to $3.0

- EBITDA to Sales was up from 14.9% to 18.4%

- EBIT to Sales was up from 12.3% to 15.8%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 10.7% to 14.8%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 7.8% to 11.5%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was up from 2.9% to 3.3%

ACTIVITIES:

Littelfuse, Inc., a global leader in circuit protection technologies, has been providing innovative solutions since its founding in 1927. Headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, the company designs, manufactures, and markets a diverse range of products, including fuses, semiconductors, sensors, and protection relays, primarily serving the automotive, industrial, and electronics sectors. Its core business revolves around safeguarding electrical systems from overcurrent, overvoltage, and other hazards, enhancing safety and reliability across applications. The fastest-growing segments include automotive electronics, fueled by the rising adoption of electric vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems, as well as industrial automation and renewable energy solutions, where demand for efficient protection components is surging. With a strong international footprint, Littelfuse continues to invest in R&D to address evolving market demands and maintain its competitive edge.

MARKET POSITION:

Littelfuse, Inc. (NASDAQ:LFUS), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Electronics company by market cap, soared $189.31 (or 75%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $441.69. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which has risen 13.3% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 61.7%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $1,868 [vs $1,594 for the NASDAQ-100 Index], for a capital gain of $826 and dividend reinvested of $42. The average annualised return to shareholders has been 36.7% in the past two years. The average daily volume of 236,584 shares so far in 2026 was 1.2 times the average daily volume in 2025.
